publishedfiledetails.title: Choose Your Spawn!
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[b]*NOTE: Please note that this mod works slightly differently if you are using Build 41 (current release) or Build 42 (current unstable beta), including which map you should be using as your reference. Please review instructions.*[/b]

Allows you to choose your specific spawn location via Sandbox Options, anywhere within the playable space.  ONLY playable in Sandbox Mode.

Go to map.projectzomboid.com, find the Cell & Relative coordinates, and enter them into the Sandbox Options when starting a new game. You will spawn in that location.

publishedfiledetails.title: Clear description for Moodles
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]Updated language description for everyone! (Hello World!)[/h1]

Considering that "More Description for Traits" exists, I was surprised nobody made a mod for moodles. I figured it would be quite handy to have this mod alongside it. So I made this little QoL mod. Enjoy!

This mod will display positive and negative effects of moodles whenever you hover your mouse over it, all it does is replace the original description with a more detailed one. All information about moodles has been tested and taken from the game’s code. If anything is wrong or missing do let me know in the comments.

[h1]Translations:[/h1]
[b]English[/b] – Chillax! + Unamelable

publishedfiledetails.title: Comically Large Spoon

[h1] "Only a spoonful!" [/h1]

Adds a Comically Large Spoon as a standalone melee weapon. It can be found very rarely in kitchens, and rarely in stores/food related crates.

Inspired by [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4JgAKW0UORo] this [/url], [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WwJe-VJ4vLY] this [/url], and obviously the original meme.

I might need to do some balance tweaks to it, I wanted the weapon to be unique but not too overpowered. It acts similarly to a sledgehammer in combat (mostly because I liked the animations), but has the utility of a shovel.

publishedfiledetails.title: Conditional-Speech
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h3]This mod generates speech utilizing a number of conditions as well as some randomization to create dynamic dialogue.[/h3]

[h3][b]Required Mods:[/b][/h3]
[quote][b]In B41:[/b]
 - EasyConfig offers options for customization.
 - Both ErrorMag and Modding Alerts are optional.
[/quote]
[quote][b]In B42:[/b]
- EasyConfig is not usable/needed, the game now has native mod options.
- ErrorMag and Alerts are required.
[/quote]

Note:[list]
[*] Color of speech can be changed in the in-game menu under "multiplayer".
[*] The game will simulate volume by grayscaling your speech. Pick a vibrant color as to tell the difference in volume.
[/list]

[b]This mod includes stock phrases for all of the moodles as well as some events.
All of them can be toggled in the menu.[/b]
Additionally, the mod applies a dynamic filter on chat depending on moodle states.
Being drunk, scared, or in pain can make you scream out or be hard to understand.

publishedfiledetails.title: Craft Helper Continued
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]Craft Helper for build 41.65+[/h1]

This is an updated version of [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2186592938]Craft Helper (41.x) by b1n0m[/url] (not required) with additional features and improved UI.

This mod will add a "Craft Helper" context menu option when right-clicking on any item with associated recipes.
Selecting that will bring up a UI with list of recipes that this item is part of, as well as ways to craft this item.
Selecting a recipe will show information about it (required items, skills etc). Result icon and required items are right-clickable.
Detailed information about interface can be found under ⓘ (info) symbol next to close button.

Apart from item tab, there is also two common tabs:
[list]
[*] Search tab, where you can find all items and recipes
[*] Favorite tab, where you can find all your favorite items and recipes
[/list]

Safe to add/remove to/from existing saves
Should be compatible with all mods and MP (except [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=3122080147]beta version[/url])

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Features:[/h2]
[list]
[*]Close window on ESC
[*]Category selector. Select desired category and filter that huge list of recipes.
[*]Window position saved to config. No more hassle with resizing/moving!
[*]Search bar. Supports custom rules (click on magnifier)
[*]Buttons to order list by name or type
[*]Ability to favorite recipes and items. Even unknown ones!
[*]Presets for favorite recipes and items.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]Options ([url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2169435993]Mod Options[/url] required to change):[/h2]
[list]
[*] Enable custom rules for search (by default ON)
[*] Show icons of items in list (ON)
[*] Show hidden items/recipes (OFF)
[*] Close all tabs on close (OFF)
[*] Select font size for items in list (Medium)
[*] Modifier keys (Shift, Ctrl) for some keybinds (all None)
[*] Show recipe origin (Mod) (ON)
[*] Show favorite items in inventories (OFF)
[*] Enable editable category selector (OFF)
[*] Delayed search (Need to press Enter to perform search) (OFF)
[*] Behaviour when right-clicking items in inventory (only "Craft Helper" option)
[*] Window opacity (80%)
[*] Scroll speed (100 ms)
[/list]
Also, with "Mod Options" you can set keybinds (Key bindings -> Craft Helper) for some actions.

[hr][/hr]
[h3]Supported languages:[/h3]
[list]
[*]English
[*]Russian
[*]Spanish
[*]Thai
[*]Korean
[*]Brazilian Portuguese
[*]Traditional Chinese
[*]Polish
[*]German
[*]Italian
[*]Ukrainian
[*]French
[*]Simplified Chinese
[/list]

If you would like to help with translation, please go to Crowdin page:
[url=https://crowdin.com/project/craft-helper-continued][img]https://i.postimg.cc/0NPptXbN/localization-at-white-rounded-bordered-1x.png[/img][/url]


[h3]Thanks to everyone who made this mod better![/h3]
[list][*][url=https://github.com/lanceris/pz_crafthelper/blob/main/CONTRIBUTORS.md]List of contributors[/url][/list]

[url=https://github.com/lanceris/pz_crafthelper]Source code[/url]

[hr][/hr]
[quote]Mod permission according to TIS policy: [url=https://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php?/topic/2530-mod-permissions/&tab=comments#comment-36478]Ask for permission[/url]
This mod can only be added to and extended with the express permission from the original creator. Having received permission, credit must be given to the original creator, both within the files of the mod and wherever the mod roams online.
If no permission is received you may not alter the mod, and it must be treated as a mod that is 'On Lockdown'.

This item is not authorized for posting on Steam, except under the Steam account [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/lanceris]Lanceris[/url], or with the copyright holder explicit permission.

One exception for the above rule is redistribution of this mod (and/or modified versions) as part of the server modpack (collection of multiple mods bundled and configured together as one workshop item).
Support will NOT be provided for users, while they are using such modpacks.

publishedfiledetails.title: Craftable Dairy Foods [Build 41]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1][b]Craftable Dairy Foods [Build 41][/b][/h1]

This Project Zomboid mod allows player to craft milk, yogurt, cheese, and butter. This mod is a designed to work on the stable branch, currently Version 41.65.

The recipes for yogurt, cheese, and butter must be learned from a new Good Cooking Magazine, which can be found in magazine shelves, mailboxes, book stores, etc. Strainer, powdered milk, and yogurt culture can be found in kitchens, groceries, convenience stores, restaurants, etc.

Cheese Preparation and Yogurt Preparation must be cooked before you can turn into usable cheese and yogurt.

Base game recipes have been updated to accept crafted milk.

[h1][b]New Items[/b][/h1]
[list]
[*][b]Good Cooking Magazine[/b] - Dairy edition, teaches recipes for yogurt, cheese, & butter
[*][b]Strainer[/b] - Tool used in creation of yogurt, cheese, & butter
[*][b]Powdered Milk[/b] - Non-perishable food item used to create milk or add to beverages
[*][b]Yogurt Culture[/b] - Non-perishable drainable item used to create yogurt
[*][b]Jar of Milk[/b] - Craftable milk using empty jar
[*][b]Bottle of Milk[/b] - Craftable milk using empty water or pop bottle
[*][b]Cheese Preparation[/b] - Craftable cheese that needs to be cooked and processed
[*][b]Yogurt Preparation[/b] - Craftable yogurt that needs to be cooked and processed
[*][b]Jar of Yogurt[/b] - Craftable yogurt using yogurt preparation, empty jar, & strainer
[*][b]Bowl of Yogurt[/b] - Craftable yogurt using yogurt preparation, empty bowl, & strainer
[/list]

[h1][b]Updated Items[/b][/h1]
[list]
[*][b]Butter[/b] - Revised to be more realistic nutrition/weight based on mod recipes
[*][b]Yogurt[/b] - Revised to be more realistic nutrition for 6 oz yogurt cup and can be used as spice in fruit or vegetable salad
[/list]

[h1][b]New Recipes[/b][/h1]
[b]Note:[/b] Note: A full jar or bottle of milk satisfies 10 hunger. If your milk has less than
10 hunger, it will not be enough to craft the butter/cheese/yogurt recipe by itself.
[list]
[*][b]Make Jar of Milk[/b] - Empty Jar + Powdered Milk (10) + Water (10)
[*][b]Empty Jar of Milk[/b] - Used to recover the jar (if spoiled for example)
[*][b]Make Bottle of Milk[/b] - Empty Water/Pop Bottle + Powdered Milk (10) + Water (10)
[*][b]Make Butter[/b] - Jar/Bottle/Carton of Milk (10) + Strainer
[*][b]Prepare Cheese[/b] - Saucepan + Jar/Bottle/Carton of Milk (10) + Vinegar (2) + Salt (2)
[*][b]Make Cheese[/b] - Cooked Cheese Preparation + Strainer
[*][b]Prepare Yogurt[/b] - Saucepan + Jar/Bottle/Carton of Milk (10) + Yogurt Culture
[*][b]Make Yogurt[/b]  - Cooked Yogurt Preparation + Strainer + Empty Jar/Bowl
[*][b]Empty Jar of Yogurt[/b] - Used to recover the jar (if spoiled for example)
[/list]

[h1][b]Updated Recipes[/b][/h1]
[list]
[*][b]Make Mildew Cure[/b] - Crafted milk (10) can be used instead of carton milk
[*][b]Make Cake Batter[/b] - Crafted milk (10) can be used instead of carton milk. Recipe also uses less sugar
[*][b]Prepare Cereal Bowl[/b] - Crafted milk (2) can be used instead of carton milk
[*][b]Prepare Muffins[/b] - Crafted milk (2) can be used instead of carton milk
[*][b]Prepare Beverage[/b] - Crafted milk (2) can be used instead of carton milk
[*][b]Create Fruit Salad[/b] - Crafted yogurt (2) can be used as spice in fruit salad
[*][b]Create Salad[/b] - Crafted yogurt (2) can be used as spice in vegetable salad
[/list]

Workshop ID: 1939995891
Mod ID: SKLDairyMod

publishedfiledetails.title: Danger Moodles
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
This mod expands on the Moodle functions to provide a more realistic challenge to wound infections, colds/flus, and mood management. (does not effect zombie infection)

ATTENTION: As of the 1.3 update this mod requires the "Mod Options" mod to be loaded as well if you want to manage individual options in a Build 41 game. There is no longer a "Moodle Keychain" item for Build 41 players. Please get the "Mod Options" mod for your Build 41 game:

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2169435993&searchtext=option


Wound infections actually need to be maintained. Any wound left unbandaged is prone to infection, even if the player cures the wound's infection then unbandages it before it fully heals. If the wound isn't dressed properly it will get infected. An infected wound will make you sick, eventually giving you a fever. A fever will either kill you or get you killed if you do not treat it properly.

Stress and Boredom now effect Morale and Sanity in a more relevant way. If your Morale drops too low then your character won't want to go on... Morale can be raised naturally by managing stress and boredom. Sanity, however, is a permanent stat for a character. You character will start to behave oddly as their Sanity drops. Once Sanity runs out, your character will be insane. An insane character is prone to insane behavior like talking to themselves, hearing whispers that aren't there, and even inflicting self-harm.

Catching a cold/flu is more dangerous now. If you let a cold/flu get too bad it will drain your endurance and make you fatigued. A late stage cold/flu will also trigger a fever.

HOW DEADLY INFECTION WORKS:
- Whenever a player character is wounded and that wound becomes infected (not zombie infected) they will begin stacking sickness.

- If a player character is wearing a dirty bandage on a wound that has not finished healing then that wound will take longer to heal.

- If a player character is wearing a dirty bandage on a wound that has not finished healing and the character is recovering from an infection sickness then the sickness will heal slower.

- If a player character is wearing a dirty bandage on a wound that has not finished healing then there is a chance that wound will become infected (not zombie infected).

- Any single wound can only become infected once. This is a result of the game's base code and not an effect of the mod itself. (Note: when a wounded body part like an arm or leg recieves another wound on top of the previous wound, the game's base code will write the newest wound over the older wound. That newer wound can get infected even if the older wound was treated.)

- Remember that bandage lifespans and effectiveness are better with higher levels of First Aid. With this mod, medic type characters are even more worthwhile.

- Remember that Disinfectant and Alcohol Wipes and the like can disinfect a single wound while eating Antibiotics will disinfect all bodily wounds together at one time.


HOW SANITY WORKS:
- Whenever a player character is in a state of medium to high Stress and Boredom they will begin to lose Sanity.

- When the character's Sanity stat is lower than 75% they will begin muttering to themselves from time to time.

- When the character's Sanity stat is lower than 50% they will begin muttering to themselves and hearing things that aren't there from time to time.

- When the character's Sanity stat drops below 15% they will begin muttering to themselves, hearing voices, arguing loudly with themselves (this generates sound that can attract zombies), and hearing things that aren't there like footsteps or zombie groans or doors opening and closing.

- The sounds an insane player character will hear depends on whether the character is inside or outside.

- Player characters do not hear sounds while they are asleep.

- Player characters do not lose sanity while they are asleep.

- When a character that has gone insane starts laughing uncontrollably or arguing with themselves loudly or screaming for the voices to stop they will generate noise that can be heard by nearby zombies.

- If the player character is not bored or stressed and as long as there is no Unhappiness moodle present a player character will slowly regenerate Sanity.



***As of v1.1 you will find a "Moodle Keychain" in your inventory. It can be used to toggle options for this mod. It was made as a key item for immersion purposes. Stick it on your key ring when you are not using it.

***With v 1.2 Danger Moodles now works with Build 40 and Build 41.



If you like what I do and want to show your support then consider checking out the Early Access of my 90's style indie JRPG "Neverspring Chronicles" here on Steam. I would be eternally greatful and could use the confidence boost!
https://store.steampowered.com/app/1747220/Neverspring_Chronicles/



Tags:Gameplay,Balance,B40,B41,Hardmode,Multiplayer,Realistic

Workshop ID: 2676526057
Mod ID: DangerMoodles

publishedfiledetails.title: Descriptive Tooltips for Skill Levels
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]Updated February 24th 2024 :[/h1]
[list]
[*] Added Russian translation.
[*] Added Korean translation.
[*] Added partial Spanish translation (~90% complete).
[/list]

[h1]Updated January 28th 2024 :[/h1]
[list]
[*] Added Polish translation.
[*] Added simplified Chinese translation.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Update: January 16th 2024 :[/h1]
[list]
[*] Added support for Artillery skill (from [u][url=]Snake's Modpack - Ammo Maker Reloaded)[/url][/u].
[*] Added support for Hunting skill (from [u][url=]Snake's Modpack - Le Gourmet Revolution)[/url][/u].
[*] Added support for [u][url=]Lockpicking skill[/url][/u].
[*] Added support for [u][url=]Driving skill[/url][/u].
[*] Added support for Archery skill (from [u][url=]Bow and arrow[/url][/u]).
[*] Translations are now OPEN.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Update: January 13th 2024 :[/h1]
[list]
[*] Added support for [u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2944004910]Woodcutting skill[/url][/u].
[*] Added support for [u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2903135820]Scavenging skill[/url][/u].
[*] Added support for Tinkering skill (from [u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2932853467]Weapon Modifiers[/url][/u]).
[*] fixed typos.
[*] made more text translatable.
[/list]

[hr][/hr]
This mod adds tooltips for each skill levels, detailing the stat gains, builds and recipes unlocked by that specific level, as well as other information/abilities relative to that skill level.

This way, new players can have an easier time learning the game, and honestly, even the majority of experienced players will learn something new from this mod.

The tooltips are displayed when hovering over a skill level with the mouse, in the skills tab of the character info window.

The info available in these tooltips is based on a combination of my own knowledge, the game's non-exposed Java files (that I learned how to decompile), the game's exposed Lua files, the wiki of PZ, and Trombonaut's videos.

If you notice a typo, or know of an effect that is not listed, or spot something that is wrong or poorly worded, please leave a comment in one of the discussions to point it out to me. I don't think I got every single effect that could be listed, and I may have made a mistake somewhere, so I'm relying on you guys for that.

This mod was made so it can easily be supported by other Skill mods, as well as dynamic trait mods.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Crafting recipes requiring Skills[/h1]
Crafting recipes unlocked from one (or two) skills are dynamically added to each skill level tooltip.
This means that crafting recipes from mods (such as Scrap Weapons for example) will also show up on the tooltip.
Crafting recipes requiring 3 or more different skills will not show up.
Also, only 10 recipes per tooltip will show up.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Buildable constructions requiring Skills[/h1]
Due to how messy the building system is, only vanilla constructions are displayed in the tooltips. Modded constructions (such as More Builds mod for example) will not be displayed.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Foraging stats & info[/h1]
For the Foraging skill, the information of what each level unlocks is taken directly from the foraging system of the game, which means it will account for potential additions or tweaks from mods (such as Soul Fisher's Foraging time for example).

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Fishing stats[/h1]
For the Fishing skill, the fishing stats are calculated with the chosen Nature Abundance as well as the current time of day and current season.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Future plans[/h1]
Support for more modded skills.
Currently working on : Driving Skill , Artillery Skill, Hunting Skill.
Will next work on : Lockpicking skill , Archery skill. Maybe Gunsmith skill .

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Translations[/h1]
I am not opening translations until this mod has been out for a week at the very least. This is so I can correct as many things as possible in the first week, otherwise the translations could be wrong.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Compatibility[/h1]
Mods that change skill tooltips will probably not be compatible with this mod.
This mod uses the ISSkillProgressBar:updateTooltip(lvlSelected) and ISSkillProgressBar:render() methods. That's it.

[hr][/hr]

Workshop ID: 3134776712
Mod ID: DescriptiveSkillTooltips

publishedfiledetails.title: DId I just Get Bitten???
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Did I Just Get Bitten?

You know that thing where you’re kiting twelve zeds, the UI is screaming, your inventory is full of canned beans, and you only notice the bite counter after you’ve already mentally filed the fight under “we totally won that”? Same.

This mod makes your survivor actually react—out loud—when something genuinely unpleasant happens: fresh bites, scratches, lacerations, and even those rude clothing tears when fashion takes a back seat to teeth.

It’s immersive. It’s slightly unhinged. It’s basically your character running a live sports commentary on their own injuries, except you’re not winning and nobody brought snacks.

Features (the serious bit)
• Overhead speech when you take a bite, scratch, or cut
• Cries for help when your outfit gets holed (somewhere between “tailor” and “preowned by a wolf”)
• Works for the local player; lines are queued so swarm fights don’t become a one-person podcast

TL;DR: fewer mystery wounds, more dramatic flair, zero additional spreadsheets.

"Stay Safe Survivors"

Workshop ID: 3693553754
Mod ID: DidIJustGetBitten

publishedfiledetails.title: Eliaz Better Bags & Backpacks B41
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[b][u]Built for version 41.53, updated for 41.65, working in MP[/u][/b]
This mod doesn't add any new item, it just change the vanilla bags and backpacks 

[h1][b]=> Bigger capacity and weight reduction for many bags and backpacks[/b][/h1] 
[h1][b]=> Bags and Backpacks weight halved / Speed penalty deleted[/b][/h1] 
[h1][b]=> [u]Special feature :[/u] you can wear a satchel with a backpack ![/b][/h1] 
[h1][b]=> you can even wear a backpack with a satchel AND a fanny pack ![/b][/h1] 
[h1][b]=> the fanny pack and the satchel have a special weight reduction of 95% ![/b][/h1] 
[h1][b]=> [u]MOD COMPATIBILITY :[/u] Brita's Armors/Advanced G.E.A.R./Scrap Armor/Better Belts/Authentic Z/S&P Military Uniform Improvements/Attach Items to Backpacks/Arsenal Gunfighter/They Knew (see details below)[/b][/h1] 


[h1][b][u]WARNING :[/u][/b][/h1] 
=> only affect new looted bags and backpacks, you have to explore new areas in order to find modded bags and backpacks (or start a new game)
=> any already spawned zombie before mod install will wear a no modded backpack/satchel !
=> if you have a bag or backpack with wrong stats (different from the table below), you can use the recipe "DELETE BAG", see below for details


[h1][b][u]Details of changes :[/u][/b][/h1]

    [/tr]

[h1][b][u]DO YOU HAVE BAGS WITH WRONG STATS ?[/u][/b][/h1] 
If you have a bag or backpack with wrong stats (different from the table above), you can use the recipe "DELETE BAG".
This recipe will appear by right clicking a bag if you have 5 Leather Strips, 1 full Thread, Scissors and Needle.
[u]=> It'll DELETE your bag so EMPTY IT before clicking ![/u]
It'll destroy your bag and spawn a fresh new bag with correct values.
The requirement of materials is here to avoid mishandlings during fights, you don't want to delete your bag instead of dropping it or equip/unequip it ;)
=> If after that your bag still don't have right stats, it's because of a mod conflict.
=> The recipe "DELETE BAG" is only available for vanilla bags and backpacks, not for other mods

All backpacks  and belts are upgraded to have same capacity/weight reduction/bag weight/no speed penalty than the backpacks from my mod. Upgrading to Tier 1, 2 or 3 is working and add more capacity.
[b][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2530389024] Shark's and Peach's Military Uniform Improvements[/url][/u][/b]
All items that act like a container have stats upgraded (CFP-90 Patrol Pack, MilitaryMedicalBag, GasMaskBag, MilitaryWebbing and MilitaryWebbingBag)
[b][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2048847068] Attach Items to Backpacks[/url][/u][/b]
All backpacks with attachments are upgraded to have same capacity/weight reduction/bag weight/no speed penalty than my mod's backpacks
[b][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2297098490] Arsenal(26) GunFighter Mod[/url][/u][/b]

[b][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2725378876] They Knew[/url][/u][/b]

[h1][b][u]My Other Mods :[/u][/b][/h1]

publishedfiledetails.title: Equipment UI - Paper Doll Equipment Interface [B42/41]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[img]https://i.imgur.com/w5yeFNP.png[/img]

[h1]Equipment UI[/h1]
Equipment UI is an addition to Project Zomboids interface that adds a paper doll (STALKER / tArKov inspired) equipment panel for managing and viewing your character's equipment.

[i][b]Use mod options to hide equipped items from your inventory.[/b][/i]

[h3][i]Part 2 - NOW AVAILABLE ?[/i][/h3]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2982070344][img]https://i.imgur.com/agDCBaI.png[/img][/url]


[h3]Drag and Drop[/h3]
Drag and Drop items to and from the equipment ui to easily adjust your equipment!
Equipment slots will highlight and change color to visualize conflicting equipment.

[h3]Customize Your Layout[/h3]
The equipment window can be detached and positioned anywhere. It will follow and collapse with the inventory when it is attached. The layout is saved between sessions.

[h3]Hotkey[/h3]
Includes a customizable control for opening/closing the Equipment UI. The keyboard control can be adjusted in the game's regular control options. The controller binding can be changed via Mod Options.
The keyboard defaults to comma.
The controller defaults to SELECT (or LS/L3 if using [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2922618524&searchtext=wookie]Wookiee Gamepad Support[/url])


[h3]Mod Options[/h3]
Install mod options to adjust the mod to your liking!
Hide equipped items from your inventory and change the scale of the Equipment UI!

[img]https://i.imgur.com/31mCw6B.gif[/img]

[h1]FAQ[/h1]
[h3]Does this work in multiplayer?[/h3]
Yes.

[h3]Does this work with modded items?[/h3]
Yes. I've accounted for many non-vanilla body slots, anything not accounted for will neatly display at the bottom of the equipment ui before the hotbar slots.

[h3][i]Part 2 - Click ?[/i][/h3]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2982070344][img]https://i.imgur.com/agDCBaI.png[/img][/url]


[h3]If you enjoy my mods, consider supporting me on Ko-fi![/h3]
[url=https://ko-fi.com/notloc][img]https://i.imgur.com/jLt3Dyv.png[/img][/url]
Or, just come check out some previews of what I'm working on next!

[h1]Other Mods To Check Out![/h1]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2901962885][img]https://i.imgur.com/XS0ayqq.png[/img][/url] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2804531012][img]https://i.imgur.com/vYXoAU6.png[/img][/url] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2903771337][img]https://i.imgur.com/gxEDz2R.png[/img][/url]

[h2]Special Thanks[/h2]
[list]
[*] dhert - Providing a comprehensive list of bodyslots and helping hide equipped items.
[*] Burryaga - Controller support feedback and testing
[/list]

publishedfiledetails.title: Fancy Handwork
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]Supports B41. Works in Multiplayer[/h1]
[h3]A new save is not required[/h3]
[h1]A VERSION FOR B42 WILL BE AVAILABLE SOON.[/h1]
[h3]There is currently a bug in B42 where Animation Sets are not recognized from the new B42 folder structure, and thus have to be duplicated which doubles size of a B42 mod. This is an issue unique to Animations. Once this is fixed by the developers (it's been reported), the B42 version will be made available.[/h3]
[img]https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hlfstr/pz-mods/devel/like_fav.gif[/img]

A Suite of Animations to make your player more alive!

[h2]Features[/h2]
[list]
[*] Attaching or Detaching an item to your Hotbar now has a chance to be done with the left arm
[*] Equipping and Unequipping your secondary item is now done with the left arm
[*] "Hand Swap" action to quickly swap the primary and secondary held items
[*] Modifier key to quickly equip items in secondary hand using from your Hotbar
[*] Animation Mask for the Left and Right Arms to correct the Positions and Rotation of the held item
[*] Includes 2 new poses when aiming a handgun and holding a secondary tool, based on your "Aim" level!  Configurable in the Sandbox Options.
[*] World Interaction animations!
[*] [b]NEW:[/b] World Interaction animations from the rear!
[*] Cooking Animations!
[*] Appropriate Vanilla Animations have now been placed on [b]over 50![/b] TimedActions that previously did not have one.
[*] [b]NEW:[/b] Wrench Repair Animation!
[/list]

[h2]Updates[/h2]
Important information is updated on this page, but full information and release notes are available in the Discussions.  Please see the latest: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2904920097/3762228679801751286/]Fancy Handwork - "It's Over 9,000!" Update[/url]

[h2]IMPORTANT[/h2]
If you receive an error about missing actions, or world interactions are simply not playing, you may need to restart Project Zomboid.  I have noticed that enabling this mod for the first time on an already-existing save and starting the game will result in the new animations not being loaded until the game is restarted. This is a [b]BASE GAME[/b] issue, but should only happen the first time.

If you receive an error following any update, please see: [url=https://theindiestone.com/forums/index.php?/topic/42925-how-to-fix-updated-mods-in-build-41-multiplayer/]How to fix updated mods in Build 41 Multiplayer[/url]
This mod adds many additional animation and action files, which may change between releases.  I try to minimize this, but sometimes it is necessary.  Unfortunately, Steam sometimes doesn't like to update these correctly resulting in errors.

[h2]Usage[/h2]
When Attaching or Detaching an item on your Hotbar, you have a chance for the action to automatically be done with your left hand.

A modifier key is added as well, which by default is bound to "Left Control".
[b]Note:[/b] By default, "Left Control" is the "Aim" key.  I personally do not use the keyboard to aim (its right-click on the mouse anyways) so I disable this keybind.  You are free to rebind the key in options.

When you press the Modifier Key and a Hotbar slot, the item slotted there will go into your Secondary Hand.  Example: (Left Control + 2) equips a Hammer to your left hand.

Pressing the Modifier and "E" will swap the items in your hands. (Configurable)

Interacting with the world normally, such as opening and closing doors and curtains, flicking light switches, and more, all now have an Animation!

Almost ALL TimedActions now have an Animation!  No longer will your character just stand there, waiting for a progress bar to fill up!

[h3]Notes about World Interactions[/h3]
If you are standing still you will turn and face the object you are interacting with if you are not already doing so. There is a Sandbox setting to control how long of a delay before you character is standing still, or the forced turn can be disabled or required.

When performing a World Interaction and moving your character will slow down slightly, and stop altogether in the case of opening a Garage Door, for a very brief moment.  I have no plans on this being a configurable option, and this is done intentionally.  It is a fantastic inclusion for immersion in my opinion, and in tight situations makes planning your movement all the more important.  After time, you will hardly notice it.
[b]NEW 01/19/2023[/b] - The speed reduction has been reduced from 50% to 25%.  This should help prevent the player from being trapped, while still being a nice addition to immersion.

[h2]Multiplayer[/h2]
Animations should sync across multiplayer with little to no issues!

[h2]Controllers[/h2]
There is currently no support for controllers, unless an action is done via the Context Menu. I will be working on Controller Support in a future Update.

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
Mouse Click World Interactions are not triggered from: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2795987309]Left Click Redux - Click To Act[/url]
Compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2915572347]The Only Cure but better[/url]. Thanks Pao!
Should be compatible with everything else.

[h3]Recommended other Mods:[/h3]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2366717227]Swap It[/url] by Chuckleberry Finn
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2706426546]Danger Zone (Dual Wield)[/url] by OgreLeg
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2651128766]Body Remodel - FINGERS.[/url] by Akyet

[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2921029369]Skizot's Carryable Boxes and Garbage[/url] - by Skizot
[b]OR[/b]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2809719515]Visible Generators and Corpses[/url] by planetalgol

Source: [url=https://github.com/hlfstr/pz-mods]GitHub[/url]

Workshop ID: 2904920097
Mod ID: FancyHandwork

publishedfiledetails.title: Filibuster Rhymes' Used Cars!
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Hey dudes, the comments are disabled because it's just Russian spam now, haha. Once build 42 is out and stable, I'll update this version and re-enable comments. Until then, if you need any help with it, feel free to join the discord. You should also check out the beta, linked below!

This version won't be updated until b42 is released in the stable branch.
If you wanna play the animation beta, though, here you go!
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3315561157

[b]Hey dudes, I'm working for TIS for now, so I don't need donations! I'm also probably not gonna have much or any time to work on this. I appreciate all y'all that helped me out! You guys got me a few more months to be able to get this done![/b]
[strike]
Hey dudes, I quit my job, learned to animate with blender and I've been working on a pretty darned big update for the mod over the last few months!
When I'm done, the mod will have...
[list]
[*]Fully animated vehicles WITH interiors, engines, even radios!
[*]Over 50 base vehicle models
[*]Over 100 variants, such as sedans, coupes, convertibles, station wagons, taxis, police, offroad versions of trucks, etc.
[*]Over 5 trailers!!! (...so like 6, haha)
[*]A few more removable parts, like bumpers and roofs, but still keeping with the vanilla spirit!
[*]A lot of bug fixes.
[*]A lot of new bugs, probably.
[/list]

I've been living off my savings and treating the mod like a full time job, but I'm pretty close to the point where I'll need to get a paying job, haha.
I really hate asking for donations, but I'm not sure I'll be able to get this totally finished up in time. If any of you guys are in a pretty good spot money wise, anything you guys toss my way will help me get more of this finished. If you don't have a lot or just don't feel comfortable donating, that's fine! I don't want you guys feeling guilty because you can't or even don't want to.
I've got a Patreon and a buymeacoffee below! Also discord, for good measure.

https://www.patreon.com/filibusterrhymes
https://buymeacoffee.com/filibusterrhymes

A few people are still reporting the mod for the picture, oopsies! If the links are hidden, my name on both is [b]filibusterrhymes[/b]. Just give that a search, homie. The donation pages also have a link to my discord.

[h1]Credits[/h1]
[b]Fenris Wolf[/b] helped out a boatload, especially with getting the old .40 version running. It probably still woulda happened, but it'd be a lot more sloppy, have unnecessary vanilla code and be less compatible.
You guys have [b]PlanetAlgol[/b] to thank for the fuel truck code, and so do I! Now you guys can fulfill your survival compound fantasy.

publishedfiledetails.title: First Aid VHS Tapes
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h2]Contents[/h2]
First Aid VHS Tapes
Adds 4 VHS tapes that can be found in VHS stores.

Each VHS tape teaches the player how to treat a different type of injury.
[list]
[*] Episode 1 - Lacerations
[*] Episode 2 - Deep wounds
[*] Episode 3 - Bone Fractures
[*] Episode 4 - Burns
[/list]

Watching these VHS tapes will also increase the First Aid skill.

[img] https://static.wixstatic.com/media/47d225_9ebe4115e6154bec987a0098884edf79~mv2.png [/img]

[h2]Background[/h2]
Not sure about you, but I've always felt that the first aid skill was too difficult to increase naturally. Whilst I am aware you can simply roll around in glass shards and treat the wounds as they appear to help boost the first aid skill, it doesn't seem very immersive... Would you do such a thing in real life to learn first aid?

While there [i]is[/i] an official in-universe way of learning first aid, you need to find 2, hard to find 'Home VHS' tapes and I've never found them in all my time playing! There is also no official, in-game way of teaching the player how to treat specific wounds. For example, crafting a splint to help fix a fracture, I only found out you could craft one by looking at the wiki...

With that said, I decided to make this mod. Something that, with a bit of searching, allows the player to advance the first aid skill whilst learning the correct medical procedure on how to treat the injuries. (The procedures being the one found on the "First Aid" page of the PZ Wiki)

I designed this mod to fit in with the other skill boosting VHS tapes such as Woodcraft and Exposure Survival. I would also consider this mod lore-friendly.

[h2]Translation Credits[/h2]
Special thanks to:
[list]
[*] SharleBu - Spanish
[*] Stryker - Polish
[*] Sknurt - Portuguese Brazilian
[*] t3qquq - Korean
[*] Degeneroach15 - Russian
[*] Suzue - Japanese
[*] Tastæ Good - Italian
[*] Vael de Lyis - French
[*] Ufzat - Turkish
[/list]

[h2]Build 42[/h2]
Mod should now be Build 42.15 compatible and viewable on the workshop mods page.

Build 41 version will be found as "First Aid VHS Tapes B41" and will still be available to those who are not on the B42 unstable version. 

Any issues with either version? please leave a comment in the bugs discussion.

[h2]Supported Mods[/h2]
[list]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2732294885] Named skill VHS tapes [/url]
[/list]

[h2]Other[/h2]
Translation requests or updates - see Translation Requests discussion.
Any bugs? - see Bugs discussion.

Do not reupload to steam or any other third party websites.

Mod ID: FirstAidVHSTapes

publishedfiledetails.title: Fitness & Workout Overhaul
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Tired of grinding fitness for hours? Want workouts that actually feel rewarding?
This mod [b]overhauls fitness/strength XP[/b] and adds [b]2 premium exercises[/b] with realistic mechanics!

[h2]⚠️ WHAT'S NEW IN 42.16[/h2]
[b] FIXED:[/b] Faced direction.
[b] FIXED:[/b] exp modifiers now fully multiplayer compatible!
[h2] WHAT'S NEW IN 42.15.3[/h2]
[b] FIXED:[/b] Instant stamina drain in multiplayer (was 2x drain on clients!)
[b] NEW:[/b] Weight-based training (heavy gear = up to 2x strength XP)
[b] NEW:[/b] Sound broadcasting (teammates hear your workout! And so do they!)
[b] NEW:[/b] Proper heat & sweat mechanics
[b] UPDATED:[/b] 20 language translations

[h2]📦 TWO MODS - USE TOGETHER OR SEPARATELY[/h2]

[h3]🏃 WORKING TREADMILL & BENCHPRESS[/h3]
Premium equipment-based exercises integrated into vanilla fitness:
[list]
[*][b]Treadmill[/b] - Needs electricity. Trains Fitness, Sprinting & Strength (with weight)
[*][b]Benchpress[/b] - Find a barbell, hit the gains. Best strength XP in the game
[*][b]Weight Training[/b] - Carry 50%+ weight for bonus strength XP (but exhaust faster!)
[*][b]Regularity System[/b] - Consistent training pays off (shown in right-click menu)
[*][b]Heat & Sweat[/b] - Cardio dehydrates you. Bring water!
[/list]

[h3]📊 FITNESS WORKOUT OVERHAUL[/h3]
Smart XP system that rewards [b]consistent, smart training[/b]:
[list]
[*] [b]Perk Level Bonus[/b] - Higher fitness/strength = faster gains
[*] [b]Regularity Bonus[/b] - Train consistently for up to +50% XP
[*] [b]Rested Bonus[/b] - Train without muscle soreness for bonus
[*] [b]Space-Out Bonus[/b] - Rest days are rewarded (promotes healthy routine)
[*] [b]Mental Health[/b] - Reduces Stress, Anger, Boredom, Unhappiness
[/list]

[h2]🎒 QUALITY OF LIFE[/h2]
[list]
[*][b]Workout With Gear[/b] - Keep backpacks on (or auto-remove, your choice!)
[*][b]Fully Customizable[/b] - 21 sandbox options to tune everything
[*][b]XP Calculator[/b] - Excel sheet included in mod folder
[/list]

[h2]✅ COMPATIBILITY[/h2]
[b]Works With:[/b] Exp Recovery ✅ | Retroactive Skill Books ✅ | Skill Recovery Journal ✅ | Dynamic Traits ✅ | Rebalanced Calorie Burning ✅
[b]May Conflict:[/b] Other mods that modify Fitness/Strength XP multipliers (let me know and I can look into making them compatible maybe)

[h2]❓ QUICK FAQ[/h2]

publishedfiledetails.title: Fluffy Hair [B41 & B42ish]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[b]This mod will add for almost all hats new adjusted hair models for the default hairs to keep their original shape when wearing something on the head. No more Ponytails or flat hairs!
You can still turn your hair into ponytail if you wish so.[/b]
(Also i improved the Ponytail(Braided) a bit to make it look more fluffy!)

Credits to "Chuckleberry Finn" to help me out with the LUA part to hide the new hair-styles in the character creation menu so you don't accidentally pick the wrong ones! [At the Old mod version of it in 41.50]
Check out his other mods:  [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/Chuckleberry_Finn/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600] Chuckleberry Finn's workshop[/url]


[h1] [b]I need you![/b][/h1]
Please give feedback if you found a bug or glitching [b]vanilla[/b] hair like so  :

[Gender] -  [Hairstyle] - [Hat]
[Additional Info]

Since there are alot of different Hair/Hat combinations i have not tested every single possible combination.
But i will write down every feedback for issues or improvements and will work through them until this mod is somewhat perfect!
(Please add Feedback in the discussion thread)


[h1] [b]FAQ:[/b][/h1]
[b]Is "Fluffy Hair" compatible with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2463184726] "Spongie's Hair Pack" [/url], [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2761200458] "Yaki's Hair Salon" [/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2456540487] "Harry's Hair" [/url] ?[/b]
I gave it a test and it seems work great together.
They also took care of making their hairs working properly with hats.
At Harry's Hair the alternative hat-hairs do show up in the hair menus for some reason.

 [b]I want to make a hat/hair compatible with your mod, how do i do?[/b]
Just Set the Hat-Category or the hair groups like that:
- Group01 If its a normal hat (CowboyHat, Tinfoil, Beany)
- Group02 If its a band (Bandana, Visor, Bandage)
- Group03 If its a hoodie
- Group04 If its over-ear (Winterhat, bicycle helmet)
- Group05 If its just default hair if you dont want any alternative hair
- Group06 If your hairs are fully covered (Shower caps, Surgeon caps)
- Group07 If its the default HatHair Style

[b]Will this mod work with a existing savegame?[/b]
Yes

 [b]Which game version works with this mod?[/b]
I have tested it with game version B41 and B42 and Hosted Multiplayer

 [b]Is this mod compatible with other Mods?[/b]
This Mod will not be compatible with mods which do:
- Change default hat items
- Change default hair styles
Mods which add completly new hairs should be compatible.

[b]Am i allowed to use this mod in a Server mod pack?[/b]
Yes

publishedfiledetails.title: Functional Appliances 2
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[b][h3]New Extractor Hoods in B42![/h3][/b]
Build them in a square where you place your generator to extract the fumes from it out of the building. All automatically, but the hood will not start until power is there and will not extract unless a running generator is under the hood in the same square.

[h3]Popcorn Machines:[/h3] They now have function and will convert instant popcorn packets and Sapph's corn kernels into edible popcorn without the pain of burning the food. Popcorn buckets are also added and you can butter the popcorn when in a bucket.

[h3]Hotdog Machines:[/h3] They also have function and will keep from burning those priceless sausages. It uses the base sausages and sliced bread in the game to make hotdogs. If you have Sapph's Cooking mod installed you can also use the sausages and hotdog buns from that mod as well.

[h3]Soda Fountains:[/h3] Are now plumbed objects and will dispense water for all the uses. It also dispenses soda to fountain cups and refills soda/water bottles for preserved use. They use soda syrups and CO2 tanks to operate. Just drop them into the counter the table top unit is on or put them inside the Slurp 'n Burp stand alone unit. These can now pull materials from any static container in the square or the square behind them. If water is a problem, try using water jugs from the Water Dispenser mod or plumb it like a sink.

[h3]Bar Taps and Kegs:[/h3] Setup the kegs in furniture containers with the taps connected and you can pour them into red cups, beer mugs, and refill bottles. Kegs pulled from any static container in the square with the bar taps. Compatible with More Brews and More Brews kegs now spawn in bar tap counters in 2.0!

[h3]Food Display Counters, Buffet Islands, Rounded Glass Display Counters and Vending Machines:[/h3] These are now temperature settable appliances for food storage. Some have Hot, Warm, or Cold others just Warm and Cold. Each segment has it's own settings. Now automatically spawn on when they can and switch to cold in ice cream parlors, cafes, bakeries, etc. Pizza parlors and other restaurants will default to warm. Soda Machines automatically default to cold.

[h3]Industrial Deep Fryers:[/h3] The deep fryers are now setup to randomly have vegetable oil in them. You can add and drain the oil. If you have power you can turn them on and fry various foods that usually need a pan of oil with Sapph's Cooking mod. A few new recipes have been added: Doughboys, Chicken Fillets, Chicken Tenders, Blooming Onions, and Fried Cheese Sticks. Added Sapph's Cooking Oil for a second option to fill the vats.

[h3]Pay Phones:[/h3] They have a minor interaction. If you touch them they might just ring but they'll attracted anyone that happens to be listening...

[h3]Wall Clocks:[/h3] They will now display the time. Clicking on them will give you the current time. Wall clocks work based on electrical availability, once off the grid they run off a built in battery that slowly drains. Power up a generator and they will recharge over time. If you are desperate, you can drop a new standard flashlight battery into them, but that's a one way trip. Clocks now show 12hr format and stop when dead. All times accurate to the minute when powered.

[h3]Paper Towel and Napkin Dispensers:[/h3] They show up as small containers and come standard with a few loose napkins. Napkins can now dry off players and wet napkins can wash off dirt and blood. In order to allow compatibility, the new ones drop in the dispensers. Vanilla game napkins have a conversion recipe to use them as well.

[h3]Coffee X-Press and Espresso Machines:[/h3] Turn on the machines and make coffee. Use mugs, teacups, and thermos or lowballs from Sapph's. Tea and instant coffee options exist in the vanilla game to make a quick fix. Add Sapph's to get coffee beans that can be ground in the espresso machine. Then you can brew coffee or espresso. Take the espresso and foam it up in the espresso machine with any "Milk" type item and turn it into a latte or cappuccino. Don't forget you need power and water. If the water is off, any source in inventory will be used for the coffee.

[h3]Bulletin Boards:[/h3] Both large and small corkboards, you can now add things into it's container and the random notices appear as overlays.

[h3]Peg Boards:[/h3] Containers that spawn with tools and have an overlay.

[h3]Pool Cue Stands:[/h3] Containers that spawn with pool cues and have an overlay.

[h3]Green Oven Silo Generators:[/h3] It has the same depletion of fuel as a standard generator and the same radius of power. It can be turned on/off manually and has an auto start feature. It can be used as a backup to other generators and sense when it's time to start. It can be repaired with electronic scrap or scrap metal but will need the generator skill, electrical 4, or mechanics 4. The standard generator manual can spawn with the machine. It has a 500 fuel unit capacity and can be filled or emptied while running. There is no need to connect these as they are stationary and already connected. If found indoors they are also vented to keep the toxins out. If they run out of fuel they will shutdown, if they run out of condition they make a loud *BOOM* that attracts zombies in the area. Now moveable in 2.0 thanks to the Green Oven Corporation! "When your power is lean, buy Green!"

[h1][b]***Fuel and Condition of generators zeros out when moving, empty first!***[/b][/h1]

v2.07 Updated for B42 Not all aspects of the original Functional Appliances are available due to changes in the game.
   Display cases, buffets, and vending machines still have temperature settings.
   Peg boards still load tools.
   Bulletin boards now have photos and fliers.
   Napkins can be used to dry or wet them to wash yourself off in a pinch. Vanilla napkins can be converted to FA
      versions.
   Beer taps work with the kegs and pour into an empty vessel of choice.
   Soda Fountains now have all the flavors available in the game, non-vanilla sodas will spawn with cans and bottles
      too.
   Popcorn machines use vanilla items to still make the popcorn.
   Hotdogs can now be made with sausage or wieners and use the vanilla buns or bread slices. All can be cooked in
      the machine.

v2.08 hotfix
   Corrected error with distribution in 42

[h1][b]***On servers you may need to set anti-cheat 21 as false if it kicks players, it points to a file mismatch between player and server.***[/b][/h1]

If you see any bugs let me know. I tried to make the functions and items as unique as possible to keep from any conflicts. A big thank you to everyone that has helped with identifying the bugs and Lazarus for his support and time fighting them on our server to try and make things right.

Thank you and enjoy!

My other mods:

[h3]All New: True Music Radio! Powered by True Music media[/h3] Now with multiplayer sync!
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3100475013

[h1]The new multiplayer true music jukebox is now available![/h1]
[b]True Music Jukebox[/b]
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3118990099
Workshop ID: 3118990099
Mod ID: TrueMusicJukebox

Need help? Find me on discord... also state your current version... they are all different...
Discord: https://discord.gg/9xNdeBr7Wz

publishedfiledetails.title: Functional Clothesline
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[b]42.13 Update [/b]

updated to 42.13 have not tested multiplayer

[hr]
-Many clothing sprites have been added, the system is a work in progress but it will match a lot of clothes or pick a random one otherwise.

-Clothing Lines are craftable. The poles can be made in the build menu, they must face eachother in the same line with a max of 10 spaces between them, then you must have
the equivalent uses of wire in your inventory to add wire.

-the poles can be dismantled if there is no wire on them

-you can steal wire from clotheslines


[hr]

[b]General info[/b]

This mod makes the clothing lines you can find into functioning containers.

the center lines have a storage size of 2 and the outside has a storage of 1, based on the sprite visuals.

added a clotheshorse. it has 6 storage and can be moved and folded. drying indoors is slower than outside

clothes drying values have been adjusted. it is now -5 every 10 minutes and -2 when inside

If it is raining the clothes will get wet at a rate of +10 every 10 minutes, which is a little slower i think than if it is on the ground.

Sprites for the lines have now been randomized. I plan to add more sprites and maybe make them match the clothing items to an extent


[hr]

[b]Build 41[/b]

The ability to craft the clothing lines has been added! this requires the mod [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3067798182&searchtext=building+menu]Building Menu[/url] . This is a soft require so if you do not desire to craft it then it will work without.

A big thanks to [b]El1oN[/b] for making this possible and also giving code to optimize and fix parts of the existing mod!.


 Added a submod for people who are adding this to a world that you have already started. When this submod is enabled you will get an option in the context menu when right-clicking on a clothesline
It will say "Make this functional", when you click it it should make the entire clothesline functional. When you are finished making the clotheslines in your world functional you can disable the mod and it should remain that way

Added submod that handles adding the ClothesLine and ClothesHorse to the BuildingMenu mod


[hr]

Thanks to [b]Jokemercy[/b] for making 3d model for the clothes horse



other mods you may like

Beds have blankets, Gun's elevators, Gun's Posters

[h1] Welcome to the Kentucky Chainsaw Massacre  [/h1]



This mod will finally add fully functional chainsaws to your zombie apocalypse. Chainsaws can be found in industrial and camping areas rarely with a spawn rate similar to sledgehammers. These powerful tools will count as "long blade" and weigh 6 points, however, they cannot be worn on your character!

Be sure to keep your chainsaw maintained with metal and refueled with gasoline.

- Does not require fresh save
- Refillable with gas
- Repairable Duct tape, Scotch tape, and Small Sheet Metal

publishedfiledetails.title: Has Been Read
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1][b]A similar feature has been added to vanilla in Build 42, showing checkmarks on books you've already read or no longer need. However, this mod is based on a different concept: "Rather than showing what you've already read, this mod shows what you *should* read next."

With this mod, unread or unfinished books are clearly marked, making it far easier to spot what to grab during looting - especially in urgent situations where every second counts.

Even in Build 42, this mod continues to offer faster recognition and survival-focused UI assistance. Use whichever system fits your playstyle - or both in combination![/b][/h1]

[h1]Overview :[/h1]
Add the unread mark on the book's icon that has not been read or has been read only halfway.
[b][i]Confirmed to work with Build 41/42 and also work on MP.[/i][/b]

[h1]Features :[/h1]
[list]
[*]Books that have not been read will display the original icon with "?". This includes skill books that are no longer needed at current level.
[*]Books that have been read only halfway will display the original icon with "!".
[*]Books that have been read completly will display the original icon only.
[*]You can mark a book. For example, you can check to see if you got it or not. Marking can be done from the context menu of the target item.
[/list]

[h1]Options :[/h1]
These options are available with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2169435993]Mod Options[/url] in B41.
[list]
[*]Target Items : Can choose which item types to show marks on.
[list]
[*]Skill Book
[*]Recipe Magazine
[*]Flier
[*]Brochure
[*]Titled Literature
[*]CD
[*]VHS
[*]Home VHS
[/list]
[*]Show Marks : Can choose which item types to show marks on.
[list]
[*]"Not Read" mark (?)
[*]"Not Completed" mark (!)
[*]"Already Read" mark (v)
[/list]
[*]Show "Current Target" mark (o) : Can specify whether to show or hide.
[*]Show "Self Marking" mark (/) : Can specify whether to show or hide.
[list]
[*]Display a mark (pencil) on marked items
[*]Display a mark (eraser) on unmarked items
[/list]
[*]Auto Marking : Automatically mark when retrieved to your inventory (including holding containers).
[/list]
[i]These options have been deprecated in v3.0.
[list]
[*]Show "Not Completed" mark as "Not Read" mark (! -> ?) : Can specify whether to change the display or not.
[*]Reverse Marking Display : Reverses the display of Self Marking (defaults to all books with Self Marking displayed).
[*]Share Flier Mark : Can specify whether to share the flier marks.
[*]Share Brochure Mark : Can specify whether to share the brochure marks.
[*]Share Titled Literature Mark : Can specify whether to share the titled literature marks.
[*]Share Self Marking : Can specify whether to  share the self marking.
[/list][/i]

[h1]Known Issues :[/h1]
[list]
[*]For CD/VHS/HomeVHS, only the "Not Read" mark will be displayed, the "Not Completed" mark will not be displayed at this time and will be treated as unread.
[/list]

[h1]Future plans :[/h1]
[list]
[*]Bug fix (if exists)
[/list]

[h1]Remarks :[/h1]
[list]
[*]Modded books should also work as long as they comply with the vanilla's book-implementation.
[*]To make the Map/Flier/Brochure/TitledLiterature already read, these items must be read after enabling this Mod. These items read before enabling this Mod will be displayed as not read. And, the read status of these items will not be transferred to other characters. While map information is retained, the read status for the map will be reset. To clear the unread mark on the map, you must read the map again.
[*]You may add it to your mod pack without confirmation and credit. However, please use at your own risk, as I have not been able to verify that it works with MP.
[*]Since v1.1, the implementation is very dependent on vanilla code. Therefore, I hope that the function of this Mod will be implemented on the vanilla side in the future.
[*][h3][b]If you were unable to log in to the MP server due to an upgrade of this mod, please try the previous versions by downloading them from "Change Notes".[/b][/h3]
[/list]

[h1]Release Notes :[/h1]
[list]
[*]v3.2 - Added "Current Target" mark to effective VHS, too.
[*]v3.1 - Hotfix for Build 42.15.
[*]v3.0 - Supported Build 42.13 MP, reorganized the options.
[*]v2.15 - Hotfix for Build 42.13.
[*]v2.14 - Supported Titled Literature, improved compatibility with Inventory Tetris (B42).
Please refer to "Change Notes" for the older history.
[/list]

publishedfiledetails.title: Herbs Spawn as Loot
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Herbs and poultices will now spawn in containers.

PURPOSE: These are hardcoded to be NEVER looted, and only foraged. It didn't make much sense that these ingredients just don't exist if you never forage, especially for cooking purposes.
The settings I run on my server are action-packed, where scripted hordes attack often, in which foraging for hours isn't desirable.

Herbs will spawn commonly in kitchen counters, refrigerators, garden stores, grocery stores, drug safehouses, and rarely on zeds.
Poultices will spawn in pharmacies, drug safehouses, clinics, chem labs, and rarely on zeds.

Workshop ID: 3059499608
Mod ID: CWAX Herbs
Mod ID: CWAX Herbs

publishedfiledetails.title: Hide
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]Hiding![/h1]
You are in a difficult situation, something is chasing you. What do you do? You hide yourself!
However, this feature is not available in PZ. This mod solves that problem.

[h1]How does it work?[/h1]
You can hide anywhere that makes sense, such as beds, wardrobes, some desks and tables, garbage cans, etc.
Cars are not an option unfortunately. To hide, just right-click on the object and click "Hide".
You can exit at any time by moving, pressing esc, etc.

[h1]Advantages[/h1]
You can hide from zombies and players, allowing you to avoid their pursuit.
This is a flexible mechanic, allowing you to hide basically anywhere!

[h1]Disadvantages[/h1]
It takes considerable time to hide, you can't do that with an attacker right next to you.
You'll also be unable to see while hidden (Barely, in SP). Lastly, hiding will give you debuffs.
Staying hidden will either bore you over time or, depending on the hiding place, freeze you out or make you sick.
Unfortunately, you cannot see how affected you are until you exit, due to a game limitation.
You can comfortably hide for 30 minutes or more without major consequences.

[h1][b]F.A.Q[/b][/h1]

[h1]Does it work in Multiplayer?[/h1]
Of course! However, this mod includes teleportation and invisibility (For Player safety).
So if you get flagged, please disable the anti-cheat.

[h1]Does it work on existing saves?[/h1]
Absolutely!

[h1]Is the mod compatible with [...]?[/h1]
This mod shouldn't have any incompatibility with other mods.
You can [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2991351207/3815159923591902337/]click here[/url] to check which mods are known to cause problems.

[h1]Can players or zombies attack me?[/h1]
No, nothing can attack you while you are hidden. In the future, I will make it so that players can manually search for it and forcibly pull you out, and zombies will have a chance to notice you.

[h1][b]Credits[/b][/h1]
Powered by [b]BitBraven[/b].
Idea by [b]Dragonk[/b].
And of course thank [b]you[/b] for supporting me!

[img]https://media1.giphy.com/media/wRhSi68hscY8xNOlXS/giphy.gif[/img]

[h1]Did you like my mods?[/h1]
Join me on Discord and maybe Youtube if you want!
I also accept Ko-Fi donations. All money is used to develop mods.

[img]https://i.ibb.co/fD7qHWV/Idle-128.gif[/img]

publishedfiledetails.title: Immersive Hunting
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Dynamic and immersive hunting mod that lets you scavange for traces of animals and then track and hunt them. It uses the foraging system combined with player's skills, traits, weather, luck and mood Once you find and pick up the traces they will spawn infront of you on the ground, from where you can further investigate and track the game (You can't take the traces/tracks with you and are forced to hunt where you foraged/investigated them. They will dissappear in 3 days if not used to hunt). [b]Works both in SP and MP and can be added/removed mid-game[/b].


[img]https://i.imgur.com/v8UuOlU.png[/img]
[code] 
[list]
[*]Four different types of animals can be tracked and hunted : Rabbit, birds, deer and wild pig.
[*]Traces of animals can be found in every zone but they most likely appear in deep forest.
[*]Small game traces can be found with foraging skill 2 and up
[*]Big game traces can be found with foraging skill 4 and up
[*]Often birds can be spotted without any skill and don't need to be tracked.
[*]Most important skills for tracking is foraging and sneaking.
[*]Traits like Hunter, Outdoorsman and Former Scout get better results while tracking.
[*]Bad weather conditions such as heavy rain, fog, snow or wind make it harder to track game.
[/list]
[/code] 



[img]https://i.imgur.com/afrrOVw.png[/img]
[code] 
[list]
[*]Hunting action will make an actual shooting sound and will attract zombies.
[*]Hunting rifles have better chance of getting a big animal then shotguns. Shotguns are better against birds.
[*]If your rifle have a scope then this helps along a lot too. Just make sure your rifle is equiped while hunting.
[*]Most important skills for hunting with a gun is Aiming and Sneaking. 
[*]Most important skills for hunting with a spear is Spear, Fitness, Strenght and Sneaking.
[*]Traits like Hunter, Outdoorsman, Desensitized, Eagle Eyed and Former Scout get better results while hunting.
[*]Traits like Clumsy and Short Sighted lower the chances a bit to succesfully hunt a game.
[*]Bad weather conditions such as heavy rain, fog, snow or wind make it harder to hunt game.
[*]When you are sick or just feel cold then this will lower the chances a bit to succesfully hunt a game as well.
[*]Hunters get mood bonus when hunting, tracking or butchering game. Hemophobic's get stress from hunting and butchering.
[*]Birds and rabbits have the vanilla nutrition value(Its is raised if you also use [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2890748284]Food Preservation Plus[/url][/b]) and [b]deer(base 17000 calories)[/b] and wild [b]pig(base 10000 calories)[/b] is from this mod. Nutrition values are multiplied randomly from 0.5 to 1.5. 
[*]Small animals give the meat you'd get from vanilla trapping and Deer/Pig will give you chunks of meat(Renamed steak, item won't change and can be used in all the recipes a regular steak could be)
[*]I did not add more bigger animals as I wanna make a fine balance between fishing , farming and trapping food supplies. Just roleplay the bears and elks were zombified as well.
[*]Works good in combination with my food resource re-balancing series: [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2886457020]Hard Forage[/url][/b], [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2909936075]Hard Trapping[/url][/b], [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2899681016]Hard Farming[/url][/b] and [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2886456626]Hard Fishing[/url][/b].

Added compatibility to [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2256623447]Firearms B41[/url][/b], [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2208315526]Bow and Arrow[/url][/b](Can only hunt rabbits with a bow) and for some weapons from [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2200148440]Brita[/url][/b].


[/list]
[/code] 

Use [b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2890748284]Food Preservation Plus[/url][/b] mod to have a lot more options on what to do with the meat. If this mod is enabled then you also get bones from big animals that can be made into glue. You can also make smoked meat, tallow and all kinds of other stuff in that mod.


[img]https://i.imgur.com/n9Hd0Zg.png[/img]
[code] 
[list]
[*]Everything is ALMOST exactly the same as in the regular version but the animals ALSO spawn in urban areas more often.
[/list]
[/code] 



[img]https://i.imgur.com/YgbKDsd.png[/img]
[table]
    [tr]
        [th][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2900671939][img]https://i.imgur.com/ASem8Pd.png[/img][/url][/th]
 [th][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2903127760][img]https://i.imgur.com/HmVDQ0y.png[/img][/url][/th]
[th][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2890748284][img]https://i.imgur.com/Nlo9bLy.png[/img][/url][/th]
[th][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2950608437][img]https://i.imgur.com/Kr7KH6n.png[/img][/url][/th]
[th][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2906633595][img]https://i.imgur.com/ol3rBLp.png[/img][/url][/th]
    [/tr]
[/table] 




[img]https://i.imgur.com/h9W20Zp.png[/img]
Russian translation - Thank you [b]axelroadman[/b]

[h1] This is part of... : [/h1]
[b][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2900697796]Project Realistic Overhaul[/url][/b] - Set of mods I've created that will overhaul the vanilla game experience to be more hardcore and true to life, but on the other hand leaves you the option to make it through and survive. It makes items and actions have more value and makes the world more alive.

publishedfiledetails.title: Immersive Overlays Retexture
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
This is a retexture  and integration of two mods:

[h1][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=533622988&searchtext=overlays]-Immersive Overlays[/url][/h1]

[h1][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2782389502&searchtext=overlays]-ImmersiveOverlaysExtanded[/url][/h1]

[b]The .lua scripts are NOT created by me![/b] I just redone the overlay textures for both of them and I couldn't find a way to replace only the textures files, both of the mods are merged in this one [u][b]SO MAKE SURE ONLY THIS MOD IS ENABLED!!![/b][/u]

[h1][b]Please, make sure to endorse BOTH of the original Immersive Overlays Mods okay?[/b][/h1]

If any of the creators of the Immersive Overlays and ImmersiveOverlaysExtanded mods want this mod down just contact me and I will remove it from Workshop as soon as possible :)

I tried to make that the overlays doesn't fill too much of the screen in a realistic/intense style.

The wet texture doesn't fill all of the screen feels like you're really wet and sweaty.
Pain, injury and bleeding gives you a intense feel like you've been trully damaged.
When you are tiered IRL doesn't feel strange and like you're tripping a little bit? That's what i tried to reach.
Hypothermia gives you an icy chilling feel and Hyperthermia gives you that burning feeling :)
When you become a zombie everything is trippy and strange.

There is a Less intrusive version too. But only enable ONE OF THEM!

I'm always open to suggestions!

publishedfiledetails.title: Improvised Glass
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
You've survived the apocalypse. You've memorized the Wood Time TV Jingle. You've stolen countless cars, survived endless nights and you've scavenged your little heart out and yet, that elusive windshield or home window eludes you. Not anymore! Introducing "[b]Improvised Glass[/b]"

Utilizing over 20 new recipes and a few new times, you can now take those destroyed glass shards you've found in the world (or smash some yourself) and utilize nothing more than your oven and a baking tray to create new glass panes.  Slather it up with some wood glue to repair existing windows, or frame it off a build a completely brand new window for your house.  Got a scalpel on hand? Perfect, now lets cut that pane down to size and give ourselves a brand new windshield or side window.

Life is just a little bit better with Improvised Glass. It is not meant to be a replacement for scavenging but more of a supplement. Maybe you already found 5 windows but the 6th is just no where to be found. No problem, build one!  Running out of decent windshields in your world? Why not just craft one and put an end to your headaches.  Just find the magazines scattered throughout the world to learn all the recipes and specialties.

[h1]TL;DR:[/h1]
Take broken glass shards, smelt them in an oven to get glass panes. Use glass panes to craft windows for your house and vehicles. Disassemble windows in a house for half the materials and in single-player you can even repair your home windows without replacing them.  Glass objects can be smashed to produce glass shards or pick them up off the ground.

[h1]Magazine List[/h1]
[table]
  [tr]
    [th][b]Magazine[/b][/th]
    [th][b]Teaches[/b][/th]
    [th][b]Requires[/b][/th]
  [/tr]
  [tr]
    [td][b]Handyman Corner's Kitchen Kilns[/b][/td]
    [td]Glass Smelting[/td]
    [td]-[/td]
  [/tr]
  [tr]
    [td][b]Woodtime: Topless in the Window[/b][/td]
    [td]House Windows[/td]
    [td]Carpentry 2[/td]
  [/tr]
  [tr]
    [td][b]Precision vehicle windows and crafting[/b][/td]
    [td]Vehicle Windows[/td]
    [td]Mechanics 2[/td]
  [/tr]
[/table]

[h1]Recipe List[/h1]
An entire directory of the available recipes is listed in the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2800412098/3273564019241681852/]pinned discussions thread[/url]

[h1]Item IDs[/h1]
[code]
ImprovisedGlass.GlassPane
ImprovisedGlass.PanWithGlassShards
ImprovisedGlass.PanWithGlassPane
ImprovisedGlass.IGMagazineGlass
ImprovisedGlass.IGMagazineWindow
ImprovisedGlass.IGMagazineCars
[/code]

[h1]Profession Additions[/h1]
[list]
  [*][b]Carpenters[/b] automatically know how to make all wooden house windows
  [*][b]Metalworkers[/b] automatically know how to make the metal house window
  [*][b]Mechanics[/b] automatically know how to make all car windows and windshields
  [*][b]Construction Workers[/b] automatically know how to smelt glass shards into panes
  [*][b]Engineers[/b] automatically know how to smelt glass shards into panes
[/list]

[h1]Regarding Crafting Windows[/h1]
It is [b]strongly recommended[/b] that you craft then install 1 house window at a time. The game has an issue where when you craft more than you can carry it will automatically drop it on the ground. However, Windows don't have a floor icon or model because they were never intended to be crafted. If you craft more than you can carry you will end up with invisible windows on the ground doing nothing.

[h1]Tested and Released[/h1]
[list]
  [*] Last tested in 41.73
  [*] Works in Single Player and Multiplayer*
  [*] Safe to add to existing worlds [i](May need to spawn the magazine)[/i]
[/list]
 [i]*Ability to repair windows currently only works in Single player[/i]

[h1]Translations[/h1]
Improvised Glass is currently supports Brazilian Portugese, Chinese, Spanish, French, Russian and Thai.  [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2800412098/3273564019241690060/]Contribute additional translations here[/url]

[h1]Source Code[/h1]
Improvised Glass is open-source and published on [url=https://github.com/Orcicorn-ProjectZomboid/ImprovisedGlass]Github[/url]
Repackage, redistribute, recode, whatever, I don't care, do as you please.
Credit would be appreciated.
