publishedfiledetails.title: Irrigation pipes for farming and water supply [B41+]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Irrigation pipes (aka Drip irrigation) for farming and water supply. Fixed for B41+

You can craft an irrigation pipe from a regular pipe, any hammer and a screwdriver.
You can put this pipe on the ground next to a water barrel, every field on which a pipe is located counts as watered.

Explanation of Smart pipes functionality (sandbox settings, default is on)

Classic pipes (aka smart pipes setting off):
After sowing the seeds, you need to manually water the fields at least once.
The irrigation system will remember this value and then automatically supply the fields with the set amount of water.
Some plants need less water and when it rains the water level rises above the set amount!
The irrigation system cannot distinguish whether the user or the rain is watering to full 100% water level.
A roof should be built over these fields.

Smart pipes:
Smart pipes will balance water levels to optimal. However after sowing new seeds, You should still water fields so health will not deteriorate too much before levels are optimal. Roof should be also build, so plants will not overflow and die from too much water (and also will lower chance of DWF).


Planned:
-auto-fertilizer - the good old function might be back)
- Vanilla metal drum support - it's not recognized as "rain collector" by base game, so it will need to have special case for this.

Pipes will work with rain collectors (modded also if do use rain collector component) but not with lakes, rivers, puddles, wells, blue barrels.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Workshop ID: 2464581798
Mod ID: waterPipes

publishedfiledetails.title: Item Stories [beta]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3569303590]B42 VERSION[/url]


[hr][/hr]


This mod makes buildings more "lived in" by spawning items inside them. It is picking what to spawn randomly from a 'story' system.

If you've tried my "'immersive barricaded start" mod , this will spawn items in a similar way, but for every building, and at a much lower chance.

I've made it into a framework, so different item stories can easily be added.

I've tested this thoughout a 20-days-long savefile, so I'm still figuring out the balance for this, but it looks good so far. I'm open to feedback. You can tweak easily the values with the sandbox settings anyway.
Start and Peak sandbox settings use the same principle as my "Time Decreases Loot" or "Time Decreases Car Condition" mods : start settings are applied before the start day, peak settings applied after the peak day, and proportionally scaled settings are applied between start day and peak day.

Other stories can easily be added, to spawn specific items in specific rooms and/or on specific furniture sprites for example.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]List of Item Story mods that I have/will release :[/h1]
[list]
[*][u][b]"Room Loot"[/b][/u] (DONE): Included in this mod. Spawns an item picked from the loot table of the room the item will spawn in. Kitchen items in kitchens, bedroom items in bedrooms, etc. Chance are low and customizable through sandbox settings, and it can trigger in ALL rooms and on ALL furniture sprites.
[*][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3037990730] Pillows on Beds [/url][/u]  (DONE): Triggers in all rooms, but only on bed sprites, and spawns only pillows.
[*][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3038100079] Propane Tanks in Garages [/url][/u]  (DONE) :Triggers only in garages and sheds, spawns only propane tanks
[*][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3038108150] ZuperCart's Supermarket Carts in Stores[/url][/u] (DONE): Triggers in a variety of stores. Low chance. Spawns Zupercart's supermarket carts.
[*][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3038476940]ZuperCart's Trolleys in Warehouses & Factories [/url][/u] (DONE)
[*][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3043616998]Pool cues and balls on billiard tables  [/url][/u] (DONE)
[*][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3123242723]Pallet crates in warehouses[/url][/u] (DONE)
[*][u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3141544193]Tire Racks in Mechanics and Garages  [/url][/u] (DONE)

[/list]

I will make an all-in-one mod with all item stories eventually.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Taking suggestions[/h1]
If you have an idea for an item story, you want an item to have a chance to spawn in some rooms or furnitures, leave it in the comments.
Or better yet, simply make a mod yourself that uses the framework. You only need to know how set up a lua table, how building rooms are named ([url=https://pzwiki.net/wiki/Room_definitions]Click here for a list of PZ's rooms[/url]), and how to check furniture sprite names using the Brush Tool of PZ's debug mode.
Though keep in mind that stories can only spawn inside a building. 'Forest' stories aren't possible, for example.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Limitations[/h1]

[b]Randomized Houses:[/b]
Item stories are only generated in rooms that have not generated a vanilla randomized house story event. So if you absolutely want Item Stories to trigger on every room, you'll need set the Sandbox Setting "Meta" -> "Randomized House Chance" to "Never".

[b]Tilesets with no surface offsets :[/b]
Some mods use tilesets that haven't properly mapped their tiles' furniture surface offsets, which means some of their furniture tiles will be considered as floor instead of furniture. Which generally means much lower chances of item story spawns.
Even some furniture in vanilla don't have their surface offset mapped. I can't really do anything about it. The devs have said they're going to implement a sudo-3d map for each sprite, but it'll be a while before they release it in a build.
These sprites can be recognized easily by simply trying to "Place Item" on one of these tiles to see that it's not working.

The mod will still work though, it's not like these tiles break anything.

[hr][/hr]

Note : Playing with this means obviously you're going to find more loot. So I recommend either turning down the vanilla loot, or turning down the Sandbox Settings of this mod.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Recommendation:[/h1]
It is recommended to use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3033301901]Loot Tables Fixed [41.78][/url] alongside this mod, but it's not strictly a must-have.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Can it spawn modded items?[/h1]
Yes. Specifically, the 'Room Loot' story can spawn them, since it picks items from the same loot tables as containers. And then there's also Zupercarts carts, for which I specifed the item Ids of the carts.
However, I am NOT responsible if another modder hasn't properly inserted their custom items in the loot tables.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Multiplayer : does it work ?[/h1]
It worked when I tested it on my own local dedicated server with only me on the server.
The framework is basically client side only, though, so I'm expecting some things to go wrong.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]What about performance?[/h1]
I made sure that when the player explores, the item stories spawner triggers on one unexplored room at a time, every tick, instead of all unexplored rooms at once. Because of this, even when I teleported into the middle of Louisville's biggest buildings, I didn't notice any lag.

[hr][/hr]

Workshop ID: 3037988451
Mod ID: ItemStories_RoomLoot
Mod ID: ItemStories_Framework

Workshop ID: 3037988451
Mod ID: ItemStories_RoomLoot
Mod ID: ItemStories_Framework

publishedfiledetails.title: KillCount
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Details kills by weapon category and by weapon in a new dedicated tab of character window.
[Solo]Adds to the genuine character panel the number of Z that died of other things than player weapon.

[h1]B42.12 Limitations[/h1]
[list]
[*]Car kills are now partially counted by vanilla. This can create counters discrepencies.
[*]Ask your questions on [url=https://discord.gg/theindiestone]Discord mod_support channel[/url].
[/list]

[h1]Technical details[/h1]
[list]
[*]Total number of kills can be exported (if selected in sandbox options) to %username%/Zomboid/lua/killcount.txt (windows path)
[*][Solo only] Counts fire kills in addition to vanilla weapon kills on the genuine character sheet.
[*][Solo & Multiplayer] Details kills by weapon category and by weapon in a new dedicated tab of character window.
[*][Multiplayer] You can visualise the kills of other players, with a delay (configurable in sandbox options).
[*][Multiplayer] You can visualise the total number of kills on the server (same delay).
[*][Multiplayer] You can deactivate the sharing of dead characters from sandbox options (to reduce GlobalModData size on never-wiping servers).
[*][Multiplayer] You can deactivate the sharing of kill count from sandbox options (to remove GlobalModData use).
[*]Visible at death time in the PostDeathUI text (can be removed in sandbox options).
[*]Handles character specific counters (saved with mod data, so server must be stopped properly for the values to be saved).
[*]Works only for one local player. i.e. not for multiscreen.
[*]Ask your questions on [url=https://discord.gg/theindiestone]Discord mod_support channel[/url].
[/list]

Credits where it is due: this is inspired from [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2451245497]Additional Kill Counter[/url]

Workshop ID: 2553809727
Mod ID: KillCount

publishedfiledetails.title: Kilometers Per Hour
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[img]https://i.ibb.co/bmLb7pQ/Banner-Features.png[/img]

[h1]Kilometers Per Hour[/h1]
Are you part of 98% of the world? Do you get confused with "MPH"? Are you annoyed that you can set temperature to celsius, clock to 24h but can't change a simple speedometer? Then this mod is for [b]you[/b]!

This mod overlays the Vanilla speedometer with a handcrafted texture that looks almost exactly the same, but now with equivalent units for KMPH. Below is a list of the numerical changes, in the following format:
[b]MPH = KMPH = As displayed[/b]
[list]
[*] 30 MPH = ~48 KMPH = 50 KMPH 
[*] 60 MPH = ~96 KMPH = 100 KMPH 
[*] 90 MPH = ~145 KMPH = 150 KMPH 
[*] 120 MPH = ~193 KMPH = 200 KMPH 
[/list]

The numbers are rounded to look more accurate like how it would be in real life, making the speedometer slightly innacurate, not that it matters much. And yes, I know, the game is set in the United States so cars wouldn't actually use KMPH.

[img]https://i.ibb.co/yQKWMxh/Banner-FAQ.png[/img]

[h1]Does it work in the Latest Build?[/h1]
Right out of the box!

[h1]Does it work in Multiplayer?[/h1]
Of course it does!

[h1]Does it work on existing saves?[/h1]
Absolutely!

publishedfiledetails.title: Light-Switch Overhaul
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]You walk over to your Light-Switch and as you flick it you think.. Why can't I change my lightbulbs? Why can't I obtain the skills needed to place a lightswitch in my own player-made base?! NO MORE! It's our Light-switch and we shall do with it what we want![/h1]
[h2]Want colored bulbs? No Problem. Battery connector? Your wish is my command. Sandbox controls? You got it![/h2]

[h3]MP Friendly![/h3]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/qtZDWr7.gif[/img]
[b][i]This mod turns all Vanilla light switches into pick-up and placeable objects, allows you to change any bulb, add battery connectors, and completely reworks lighting from the vanilla game. It even adds the ability to craft right and left-sided switches! Each side (right and left) of the light switch faces in any direction depending on the wall it's touching. You can disable any feature via sandbox settings, some of which change on the fly, and most require a restart. I suggest setting sandbox settings, then restarting your server immediately.[/i][/b]

[img]https://imgur.com/VhyjlvW.gif[/img]
[h3]This mod can be added or removed from any save, but note that any moved/changed switches will remain even if the mod is uninstalled.[/h3]
[b]To craft, find the book in the world, read it, and have the required tools. Other requirements are set via sandbox options.[/b]

TODO: Customize Crafting Speed by Sandbox Option, Add UI to change light strength range, and other various features.

Currently Thai and Brazilian Portuguese Translation. You can check posts and submit translations if you'd like. There is a lot to translate, and I don't have the time myself.

[h3]Huge thanks to many from the Official PZ Mod Development Discord, including Aiteron from The Indie Stone![/h3]
[b]Here are the individuals who went above and beyond to help make this mod possible. I highly recommend checking out their mods![/b]

Made the equippable lightswitch models!

[h1]FAQ[/h1]

[b]Q: "Can this mod be used in an existing world\save?"[/b]

publishedfiledetails.title: Map Symbol Size Slider
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
This mod adds a symbol size slider to map symbols menu. With it you can change the size of symbols and notes on the map.

[h2] Features [/h2]
[list]
    [*]Resize symbols and notes on global map and map items
[/list]

[h2]FAQ[/h2]
[h3] Can I make the symbols even bigger? [/h3]
The main purpose of the mod is to add the ability to make them smaller or slightly bigger. [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2734705913/4299250686372317431/]Here is how you can do it manually[/url], if you really want to.

[h3]Can this be added into an existing save?[/h3]

So if you manage to reproduce this issue, please report it in the comments so I can fix it (if the issue is in my mod). That would be highly appreciated!

publishedfiledetails.title: Medicine Moodles
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h2]Works on 41.78.16 SP/MP[/h2]
[h2]Safe to add to/remove from existing saves[/h2]

I just took a painkiller. When should I take the next one?
Medicine Moodles will help you know the duration of pills.
Moodle Level 1: 0%-10% remaining time
Moodle Level 2: 10%-40% remaining time
Moodle Level 3: 40%-70% remaining time
Moodle Level 4: 70%-100% remaining time

[h3]Here are some notes.[/h3]
Antidepressants need some time to take effect.
Drinking alcohol gives a combination effect of Antidepressants, BetaBlockers, Painkillers and less effective SleepingTablets.
Intensity of SleepingTablets stacks. Hover your mouse over the moodle icon to check.
LemonGrass has an internal cooldown for reducing food sickness or poison level.
Other drug mods using vanilla mechanism should be shown as well.

Enable MedicineMoodlesSkillRequirements submod if you want to add skill requirements for moodles instead of showing unconditionally.
The values can be changed in sandbox options.

Workshop ID: 3093700684
Mod ID: MedicineMoodles
Mod ID: MedicineMoodlesSkillRequirements

publishedfiledetails.title: Mini Health Panel [B41/B42.13+]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h2]✅ The mod is working fine on [u]B42.15.1[/u] (unstable multiplayer) ✅[/h2]
[h1]🔄️ [u][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3622692490]B42.12 version here[/url][/u] (before unstable multiplayer update) 🔄️[/h1]

[hr]

A minimalistic wounds display without the need to open the health panel. Can be disappeared when no wound need to be treated.

[img]https://i.ibb.co/vZ2Lghv/gif-mod.gif[/img]

[h1]Updated for Build 42 [/h1]

The add-on has been [b][u]updated for Build 42[/u][/b] while a separate version for [b][u]Build 41 remains untouched and fully compatible[/u][/b], thanks to a new files structure system where each version of the mod is only visible by its respective game build, ensuring seamless functionality for players on both versions.

[b][u]Only for B42[/u][/b], It now includes a new muscle strain indicator for each body part. The indicator evolves by becoming less transparent and changes color from orange to red as muscle strain worsens.

If auto-hide is enabled, the silhouette is revealed with a brief blinking effect when a strain threshold is reached.

This feature can be toggled off if not needed.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/BnjPXPr.gif[/img]

[h1]What does it do?[/h1]
It give a permanent overview of your character's wounds on a minimalistic display. The body silhouette disappear when there are nothing to treat and appear when there is a wound.

When a body part is flashing red, there is a wound that needs to be treated.
When it's white, the wound is either bandaged or splinted.
When it's orange, the bandage is dirty and needs to be replaced.

If the wound is bandaged but it's still flashing that means you didn't fully treated the wound (glass still lodged, deep wound needs to be stitched, etc...)

[h1]How to use it?[/h1]
Right clicking on the component will bring a contextual menu with all wounds and the settings of the addon.

Left clicking just brings up the classic health panel of the base game.

You also can drag it around and place it where you desire.

[h1]Optional mod[/h1]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2169435993]Mod Options[/url] is optional and adds an option to disable my mod (mainly for public servers)

[h1]Compatibility[/h1]
This mod should work with any other mod (no incompatibility has been reported so far)
This mod works in single and multi-player.
This mod doesn't work in split-screen and won't be.

[h1]Known issue[/h1]
[list]
[*]The panel may look small or big depending of your game resolution, this is unfortunately a common problem for most of interface add-ons.
[list]
[*] This may be looked on when I will have the time.
[/list]
[/list]

[h1]Support me[/h1]
If you really like my mod you can support me through [b][u][url=https://ko-fi.com/speedy_von_gofast]Ko-fi[/url][/u][/b]

Workshop ID: 2866258937
Mod ID: MiniHealthPanel

publishedfiledetails.title: Mod Options (Build 41)
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
ModOptions WIP (paused)!

Allows other mods to be customized.
Any mod author can add custom options to their mod.

[h1]Requirements[/h1]
(!) THIS MOD IS COMPATIBLE ONLY WITH BUILD 41
(!) For Build 42 see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3386860561]here[/url].

[h1]Steam Workshop Issues[/h1]
1. Quit the game.
2. Unsubscribe. Wait ~1 minute.
3. Subscribe. Wait ~1 minute.
4. Make sure the mod version is [b]1.4.4[/b]

[h1]Minimal Code (for modders)[/h1]
Put it in subfolder media/lua/client of your mod folder.
[code]
-- These are the default options.
local OPTIONS = {
  box1 = true,
  box2 = false,
}

-- Connecting the options to the menu, so user can change them.
if ModOptions and ModOptions.getInstance then
  ModOptions:getInstance(OPTIONS, "MyModID", "My Mod")
end

-- Check actual options at game loading.
Events.OnGameStart.Add(function()
  print("checkbox1 = ", OPTIONS.box1)
  print("checkbox2 = ", OPTIONS.box2)
end)
[/code]
This mod should be enbaled.
But note that your mod will work without this mod!
So you do not have to create a hard dependence on this mod.

For more info, see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2169435993/4260919351480715709/]Guide[/url].

TODO:
- [strike]Options other than checkbox[/strike] (done)
- Option like edit input.
- Option like change color.
- Possibility to add custom tab
- Possibility to tune by server admins only (using UI)
- [strike]Sandbox options.[/strike] (done)

publishedfiledetails.title: Mod's Bulk Items

This mod allows you to put a few different food and drink items into functional boxes that all have unique models and icons to fit.
This came about when i had an idea that it would be really neat to have some items in actual "packaging" that serve a function and are nice and decorative. Additionally, they spice up looting since there is a small chance to find an entire case of beer, a box of chips, or another item that will help more than a single item would.
It additionally lets you pack any of said items into their respective "packaged" forms for better weight reduction, or to neatly decorate your safehouse in a neat way.
(and yes, i am that guy who made Improved Spears and the Dash Roamer, i do other things)

PLEASE NOTE:
Use the "submod ModsBulkFoodItems" if you would just like to add the food items, ignoring changes to cigarettes this mod does which may cause problems when added to an existing game/server

Items currently supported are:
All Vanilla Alcohol Items (Beer, Wine, Bourbon, etc)
All Soda Cans
Granola Bars, Chocolate, Jujubes, Gummy Bears, Gummy Worms, Candy Fruit Slices, Jelly Beans, and Chips
Processed Cheese, Steak, Pork Chops, Mutton Chops, Ground Beef, Meat Patties, and Chicken can be found in preserved packaging that will keep them preserved.
And one last item, not really food related but a friend asked for it, A Large Gas Can.
The Large Gas Can holds 2.5x more gasoline than a normal gas can, but must be used 2-handed, and is rarer when looting.

PLANNED ITEMS:
Bulk boxes for Orange Soda bottles and more of the "Sweets" food section. Possibly a larger, 2 Liter bottle that can be refilled too.

publishedfiledetails.title: Moodle Framework B41
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Makes it easy to add moodles for modders.


[h1]Moodle Framework Configuration[/h1]
If you want colors to be easier to distinguish, you can set to white the reference color for moodles.
This applies to both vanilla and modded moodles.
You can also deactivate various displays to enhance mod compatibility.
Modded Backgrounds with small extensions right or down are now handled too.


[h1]How to create a new moodle[/h1]
If you wanna create a Moodle * then follow the next steps, replacing * by your moodle name.

[h2]1/ Moodle Texture[/h2]
Add Moodle *.png under media\ui.
Should be 30 by 30 pixels png with alpha enabled.

[h2]2/ Texture[/h2]
Add the following text under media\lua\shared\Translate\EN
[code]Moodles_EN {
    Moodles_*_Good_lvl1 = "text_1",
    Moodles_*_Good_desc_lvl1 = "text_desc_1",
    Moodles_*_Good_lvl2 = "text_2",
    Moodles_*_Good_desc_lvl2 = "text_desc_2",
    Moodles_*_Good_lvl3 = "text_3",
    Moodles_*_Good_desc_lvl3 = "text_desc_3",
    Moodles_*_Good_lvl4 = "text_4",
    Moodles_*_Good_desc_lvl4 = "text_desc_4",
    Moodles_*_Bad_lvl1 = "text_1",
    Moodles_*_Bad_desc_lvl1 = "text_desc_1",
    Moodles_*_Bad_lvl2 = "text_2",
    Moodles_*_Bad_desc_lvl2 = "text_desc_2",
    Moodles_*_Bad_lvl3 = "text_3",
    Moodles_*_Bad_desc_lvl3 = "text_desc_3",
    Moodles_*_Bad_lvl4 = "text_4",
    Moodles_*_Bad_desc_lvl4 = "text_desc_4",
}[/code]
You can implement only a subset of this as long as you call only the implemented part.

[h2]3/ Moodle object creation[/h2]
Add these lines in your mod:
[code]require "MF_ISMoodle"
MF.createMoodle(*);[/code]

[h2]4/ Moodle value updates[/h2]
Access your moodle from your code with
[code]require "MF_ISMoodle"
MF.getMoodle(*,playerNum):setValue(myNewMoodleValue)--float[/code]
0.0 <= myNewMoodleValue <=1.0
Missing playerNum will make the mod incompatible with SplitScreen.

[h2]For tuning options [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2859296947/3380536561724844838/]see dedicated discussion[/url][/h2]


[h1]Limitations[/h1]
[list]
[*]Not tested with multiplayer. (it is expected to work)
[*]Not tested with splitscreen. (it is expected to work since last version)
[*]Oscillations are not based on vanilla value and the behaviour may depend on performances.
[/list]


[h1]Credits where it is due[/h1]
[list]
[*]This is inspired from the (removed) mod [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2665369300]Zombie Camouflage[/url] moodle management by the awesome [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/Dislaik/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]Dislaik[/url].
[/list]


[h1]Known mods using this[/h1]
[list]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1299328280]More Traits[/url]
[*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2584991527]Auto Sewing[/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: More Description for Traits [b41 & b42.8]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]42.13+ version is in a separate mod:[/h1]

[u][b][i][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3623919908]Click here for the B42.13 version ![/url][/i][/b][/u]

[hr][/hr]

This mod is intended for players who are confused about what effects each trait and profession has on the character.

It adds description to the traits that need it to describe their effects on the character in detail, instead of leaving people guessing with vague sentences. This way, players can have an easier time imagining their own builds by themselves.

It also adds the description on mouse hover in the character's info screen, in game. This works with descriptions of traits from other mods too, like Banjo's item kits or Dynamic traits for example.

This is based on a combination of my own knowledge, the game's non-exposed Java files (that I learned how to decompile),  the game's exposed Lua files,  your feedback, and the wiki of PZ.

Most of what I added in the traits' descriptions are in parenthesis so you can distinguish what is vanilla description and what is not.

If you notice a typo, or know of an effect that is not listed, or spot something that is wrong or poorly worded, please leave a comment to point it out to me. I don't think I got every single effect that could be listed, and I may have made a mistake somewhere, so I'm relying on you guys for that.

[hr][/hr]
[h1]Compatibility[/h1]
Works with build 41, in multiplayer b41. DOESNT WORK with 42.8.
It should be compatible with any mod that adds traits or skills.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Translations[/h1]
B41 translations are CLOSED.
B42 translations are open , but be advised that it risks becoming obsolete soon as B42 gets more matches, and I will most likely take a couple weeks to add the translation.

Anyone is welcome to volunteer to translate to their language.

[hr][/hr]
[h1] Foraging stats:[/h1]
The mod details the foraging stats bonuses for each trait and profession.
Stats are : Foraging radius bonus, Weather Effect reduction, and Darkness Effect reduction and also Specialisation bonuses for every foraging category.
All foraging stats are added dynamically, which means any balance changes will be automatically updated, and other traits that get foraging stats in their descriptions (if they have foraging stats).
Source file: media/lua/shared/Foraging/forageDefinitions.lua

[hr][/hr]
[h1] Character Info Screen Tooltip:[/h1]
The mod displays the detailed description of each trait in the character's info screen window on mouse hover. Works for professions too (except Unemployed since it doesn't have an image).
Works with traits of other mods too! Although their descriptions probably won't be as detailed.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Dynamic Free recipes[/h1]
Free recipes have been dynamically added to each trait and profession description, which means any future free recipe changes in vanilla will automatically show up on this mod, without needing to update the mod.
Modded traits will also have their free recipes added to their description, just like the foraging stats. And modded recipes will also dynamically show up if they are given for free to a trait or profession.
As of 42.8 I limited the number of recipes that can show up on one trait.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Mod Options[/h1]
There are now 3 mod options :
[list]
[*]Show more description 
[*]Show Free Recipes
[*]Show Foraging Stats
[/list]
So you can now disable the things you don't want to see.

[hr][/hr]

[h1]Give your feedback[/h1]

I've tried to be as exhaustive as possible, but I'm sure I've made some mistakes or interpreted things wrongly here and there. Please tell me in the feedback & bug reports discussions , or just leave a comment.
[hr][/hr]


Workshop ID: 2685168362
Mod ID (b41): MoreDescriptionForTraits4166
Mod ID (b42): MoreDescriptionForTraits42

publishedfiledetails.title: More Smokes
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]MORE SMOKES - CANNABIS AND TOBACCO[/h1]
A large effort was put into the development of this mod to put out a high quality production that functioned effectively to bring you the full experience.

This experience includes the following Mechanics:[b]
[list]
  [*]FARMING
  [*]DRYING & CURING
  [*]PROCESSING PRODUCTS
  [*]USABLE TOBACCO
  [*]USABLE CANNABIS
  [*]EDIBLES
  [*]PIPES/HOOKAHS/BONGS
  [*]CUSTOM CULTIVATION SKILL
  [*]CUSTOM TRAITS
  [*]STONED MOODLE
  [*]FLEXIBILITY THROUGH SANDBOX OPTIONS
[/list]
[/b]
[h1]FARMING[/h1]
Grow tobacco and three different strains of Cannabis
[img]https://i.imgur.com/VETpK80.png[/img]

[h1]PROCESSING[/h1]
After cultivating your crops to a harvestable stage. You can work through the simplified drying and curing process. Once that is completed you will get some products you can then make into smokable or edible options.
[img]https://i.imgur.com/apPwcUA.png[/img]

[h1]USABLE PRODUCTS[/h1]
With processed goods, rolling papers, or tobacco wrap you can make yourself a wide arrangement of things that fit your stoner needs.
[img]https://i.imgur.com/s80EKnD.png[/img]

[h1]PARAPHENALIA[/h1]
One of the easy routes is taking your buds directly to some glassware. It removes the step of needing to wrap things but you are going to use a little bit more loose materials
[img]https://i.imgur.com/c6wLjuZ.png[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/Iu08SEF.png[/img]

[h1]STONED STATUS[/h1]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/22lmnBy.png[/img]
All Cannabis products have the ability to increased your stoned stat. As it increases you will become more stoned. This status will decrease over time. Factors that impact this are in game settings / length of day / this mod sandbox options. [i]Will require the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/?id=2859296947]moodle framework[/url] for core features [/i]

[table]
[tr]
[th] [h3]POSITIVE BENEFITS[/h3] [/th]
[th] [h3]NEGATIVE IMPACTS[/h3] [/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
[td][b][list]
  [*]Reduction of Boredom over time
  [*]Reduction of Unhappiness over time
  [*]Reduction of Stress over time
  [*]Reduction of Pain over time
[/list]
[/b][/td]
[td][b][list]
  [*]Will increase thirst over time
  [*]Will increase hunger over time
[/list]
[/b][/td]
[/tr]
[/table]

[h1]Custom Traits[/h1]

[table]
[tr]
[th] [h3]STONER[/h3] [/th]
[th] [h3]PARANOID TOKER[/h3] [/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
[td] [img]https://i.imgur.com/8prRwzY.png[/img] [/td]
[td] [img]https://i.imgur.com/BMcba4K.png[/img] [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
[td][b][list]
  [*]+1 to cultivation skill starting out
  [*]Learn all basic recipes
  [*]20% increase on stoned benefits
  [*]20% reduction of thirst and hunger rates while stoned
  [*]Will lose stoned status more quickly
[/list]
[/b][/td]
[td][b][list]
  [*]Will gain stress instead of losing it when consuming cannabis
  [*]Gains a large chunk of panic when consuming cannabis
  [*]When stoned panic reduction is decreased making it last longer
  [*]Will not receive the benefit of stress reduction while stoned
[/list]
[/b][/td]
[/tr]
[/table]

[h1]Sandbox Options[/h1]
One of the important factors was the flexibility to use this in most environments. It was important to allow you to manage your loot and spawn rates plus configure the speed/strength of the stoned status.[b]
[list]
  [*]Loot Distribution is managed by (Loot Setting * Spawn Multiplier * rarity). Rarity can be found in the loot lua file but is between 1 and .01

  [*]Stoned Increase and Decrease will specifically manage the value change amount over time. This is mostly to accommodate time settings. 1 hour standard fits the default 2 value. If you played a 2 hour setting then 4 would be best. In the end its up to you

  [*]Munchies multiplier impacts the amount changes to thirst and hunger stats while under the stoned status

  [*]Stoner Perk and Paranoid options impact the custom trait influences while under the stoned status

  [*]Rare magazines are for stronger variants. These are meant to be read only once so they will be removed from your inventory after learning the recipe

  [*]Ability to change the amount of harvest gained

  [*]Influence the time it takes to grow, rot, or dry, these settings will require a restart and will only impact new items

  [*]NEW OPTION to turn off natural spawns of tobacco or cannabis items. This allows for removing cannabis and keeping only tobacco or the opposite of only cannabis and no tobacco. It influences the items in the smokes kit too.
[/list]
[/b]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
The mod was tested in various environments but there is no possible way to accommodate every single mod or server setup. There should be no expectations for cross compatibility on a larger scale among the pool of mods for this game. On that note, much of the content was intentionally designed to fit its own module well but other farming mods and other mods that influence the onEat Cigarette function may be impacted (how and to what degree is unknown)[b]
[list]
  [*]More than likely not compatible with other farming mods, would encourage you to avoid any that overwrite vanilla mechanics
  [*]B41.78+ Single Player / Multiplayer Compatible
  [*]You can influence mechanics directly through sandbox settings
  [*]Will require the Moodle Framework for core features to work
[/list]
[/b]
[hr][/hr]
[img]https://steamuserimages-a.akamaihd.net/ugc/1660101375273576639/0C12E325C464F5DC166E5FF2AB70A43581BED64F/[/img]

I appreciate your interest in my mod, please don't forget to rate it. If you feel especially inclined, donating a [url=https://ko-fi.com/ryanhayes]coffee[/url] goes a long way for me.

[url=https://ko-fi.com/ryanhayes][img]https://i.imgur.com/2uTskWl.png[/img][/url]

[td][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2884033891][img]https://steamuserimages-a.akamaihd.net/ugc/1859450874949341016/318A3B805ED8A0AC6BC9008D3BCDBFA61862872B/?imw=200&imh=200&ima=fit&impolicy=Letterbox&imcolor=%23000000&letterbox=true[/img][/url][/td]
[/tr][/table]

This mod has come to fruition due the wonderful grace of supporters and the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/linkfilter/?url=https://discord.com/invite/projectapocalypse]Project Apocalypse Community[/url],

thank you CollectableCat, Ghost Recon, ChiefBacon, A_Secret_Lizard, Combo, Javi-Juice, Altah, Ulamog, Jagata, Presage, ADC1987, Saltyminx, Zero1st, Vernage, Wild, Zeter, NeonScreams, and OwenFlory.

Of course nothing would be possible without this game so thank you The Indie Stone

get it functional for more people.

publishedfiledetails.title: More Zombie Death Animations (B41)
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Have you ever felt like cutting down a horde of zombies felt a little underwhelming? Perhaps seeing the same death animation repeat over and over again took away some of the fun and enjoyment that you would have otherwise gotten. Thanks to this mod, cutting down hordes of zombies will be as exciting as ever, featuring both quick and dramatic death animations to enhance your zombie slaying experience.

Features:
-Changes and adds various zombie death animations utilizing unused developer assets. (five in total)
-The animations are tied to a zombie's walk animation, allowing for more animation variety than in vanilla.
-Works with melee, ranged, and even throwable weapons
-Works with zombies who die to fire and explosives
-Compatible with popular animation mods such as Authentic Animations, True Actions, and
 Fancy Handwork
-Should be fully compatible with any mod that doesn't make changes to the zombie death animations or the zombie falldown and walktoward folders

[b]Important[/b]: This mod works best when zombie speed is set to fast shamblers (default) or sprinters. Zombies set to shambler speed will only have three out of the five death animations.

Works with multiplayer.  Server-sided.
Safe to add or remove from an existing save game.

[b]Information Regarding Build 42[/b]: 

Hello everyone,

First of all, I just wanted to thank all of you for the support and kind words in the comment section. I'm glad my mod was able to bring enjoyment to your various Zomboid playthroughs. As I have stated previously, I was originally planning to update this mod to build 42 as long as the developers didn't make any major changes to the animation system.

However, during my brief testing of build 42's unstable build, it seems that the developers have completely overhauled the part of the animation system that I made modifications to. Their new system seems to take both the weapon type and swing direction into account when a zombie is killed, making my mod inferior to their new system. Additionally, the animation rework that the developers introduced in build 42 was actually my original goal when making this mod. 

It is for these reasons that I have decided to not update this mod to build 42. It will stay on the Steam page for those of you who prefer to play on build 41, but it will not receive any further updates.

Thanks again for the support, and have fun in build 42!

Workshop ID: 3147442677
Mod ID: More_Zombie_Death_Animations

publishedfiledetails.title: MorePlushies!
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]More Plushies[/h1]
Expand your plushie collection with a large set of new collectibles, each functioning with the attachable plushie slot on bags that have this attachment option.

[b][url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/108600/Project_Zomboid/]Build 42[/url] is now fully supported.[/b] Both B41 and B42 are actively maintained.

[h1]What's Included[/h1]
Over 80 new plushies across four rarity tiers: Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Very Rare. Includes fan favorites, original designs, and pop culture references. Also includes the [b]Plushie Collection Binder[/b] which lets you track every plushie you've ever picked up. Craft one from basic supplies you can grab, or find one in the world.

[h1]Where Do They Spawn?[/h1]
Plushies are distributed across toy stores, daycares, classrooms, children's bedrooms, and general storage locations. Rarer tiers appear less frequently and in fewer location types. Spawn rates and location categories are fully configurable in sandbox settings.

[h1]Sandbox Options[/h1]
Control overall loot frequency, toggle each rarity tier independently, adjust spawn rates per tier, and enable or disable specific location categories. Set everything to off for zero spawns with no residual loot paths.

[h1]Is It Safe to Add or Remove?[/h1]
No vanilla items were modified. The mod can be added to an existing save safely. Removing it mid-save will cause any mod items in the world or player inventory to disappear but as always you should back up your save first.

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
Special thanks to Authentic Peach, author of Authentic Z, for the inspiration for plushie attachments.

publishedfiledetails.title: Multiple Generators
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]Multiple Generators[/h1]
Combine power of multiple generators.  With this mod, generators can work in parallel, so that they consume less fuel.  Game will recognize what items are within range of more than one generator and split fuel usage across them proportionally. Check how generator info data changes when you turn on more generators.

Finally your frozen rabbits are safe and you can safely go scavenging.

As an extra feature, stove power consumption depends on temperature setting.  Probably more fine-tuning options will be added later. 

Generator info window is slightly more neat so that you can easily see the list of devices on their power usage. 

Additionally you can see a graph for historical data of power usage. If you you solar panels mod then you can see the effect of the failsafe that was triggered. Also check my [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3209239259]sensors mod[/url] that introduces a lot of house automation related to lights and other devices that help you conserve fuel and improve your life in other aspects. 

Works with Generator Time Remaining and Generator Range mods.
Can be added mid-game. Works single and multiplayer.

If you like my work:
[url=/ko-fi.com/M4M7SGR4P][img]https://storage.ko-fi.com/cdn/kofi2.png?v=3[/img][/url]

publishedfiledetails.title: Nihilist's Silencers
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Introducing silencer attachments for specific firearm calibers, obtainable in the world or through crafting. They vary in effectiveness by caliber, such as 25% noise reduction for shotguns and 40% reduction for 9mm pistols by default. They're intentionally scarce and challenging to craft to maintain game balance, but you can freely customize them to suit your preferences.

Currently, it supports [b]all base game firearms[/b] (exceptions: Sawn-off and Double Barrel Shotguns) and several mods such as [i]Vanilla Firearm Expansion[/i].

[h3]★ Can be added/removed mid-save![/h3]

[h3] [b]Important[/b], you [i]only[/i] need to enable [b]"Weapon Sound Only - For Addons"[/b] in game mods menu for this mod to work. You DON'T have to enable main "Weapon Sound Expansions" mod.[/h3]

[h1]Added Firearm Attachments[/h1]
[list]
[*] Silencer (.22)
[*] Silencer (9mm)
[*] Silencer (.44 Magnum)
[*] Silencer (.45 ACP)
[*] Silencer (.38 Special /.357 Magnum)
[*] Silencer (.223 / 5.56x45mm)
[*] Silencer (.308 / 7.62x51mm)
[*] Silencer (Shotgun)
[/list]

You need to have a screwdriver to attach these.

[b]Important[/b]: you cannot use a silencer designed for one caliber on a weapon with a different caliber. However, you can convert between them using conversion recipes.

[h1]Supported Mods[/h1]
[list]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2667899942]Vanilla Firearm Expansion (B41)[/url]
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2873405334]Gunrunner's Weapon Pack (A VFE Expansion)[/url] 
[*] [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2893333090]VFE - S.T.A.L.K.E.R pack[/url] 
[/list]

[h1]Sandbox Customization (all the details, [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3054443004/3882724699526957719/]Here[/url])[/h1]
▸ for each silencer attachment
[list]
[*] change [b]effectiveness[/b] in % [i](See [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3054443004/3882724699526957719/]Here[/url] for further details)[/i]
[*] increase [b]base sound radius[/b] [i](Default, 0)[/i]
[*] enable/disable [b]crafting recipe[/b] [i](Default, true)[/i]
[*] change [b]loot rarity[/b] [i](See [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3054443004/3882724699526957719/]Here[/url] for further details)[/i]
[/list]
▸ for crafting and conversion recipes
[list]
[*] adjust [b]metalworking[/b] level requirement for crafting recipes [i](Default, 6)[/i]
[*] adjust [b]aiming[/b] level requirement for crafting recipes [i](Default, 2)[/i]
[*] enable/disable all [b]conversion[/b] recipes [i](Default, true)[/i]
[*] adjust [b]metalworking[/b] level requirement for conversion recipes [i](Default, 5)[/i]
[*] adjust [b]aiming[/b] level requirement for conversion recipes [i](Default, 2)[/i]
[/list]
▸ miscellaneous settings
[list]
[*] adjust [b]base loot modifier[/b] for all silencer types [i](Default, 1.0)[/i]
[*] enable/disable [b]multiplayer sound radius limitation[/b] [i](Default, true)[/i]
[*] enable/disable [b]overriding base firing sounds[/b] [i](Default, false)[/i]
[/list]

[h1]Next[/h1]
[list]
[*] make [i]Weapon Sounds Expansions[/i] mod optional ? I must find good sound effects tho...
[*] make some of the silencer models smaller
[/list]

[h1]Credits[/h1]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561199178116693/]Satispie[/url] for the sound mod!

publishedfiledetails.title: On the Door
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1][b]In Build 42, keys no longer spawn on doors. However, the logic for obtaining keys from doors remains intact. Therefore, this mod has been updated to be compatible with Build 42 in case door key spawning is reintroduced in the future. It's difficult to check the implementation frequently, so if you can confirm that spawn on the door have been restored with this mod for B42, please let me know![/b][/h1]

[h1]Overview & Features :[/h1]
Car keys spawn in the ignition, glove box, buildings near the vehicle, on the ground, and on zombies, but there is also a low probability that they will spawn on the "vehicle door". However, when it spawns on a vehicle door, there is no way to confirm that it has spawned, and it seems that the key can only be retreived under certain conditions.

Therefore, this mod will add the feature to automatically retrieve the keys from the door and notify the player with a message when the player attempts to enter a vehicle whose keys are spawned on the door, regardless of the conditions.
[b][i]Confirmed to work with Build 41/42 and also work on MP.[/i][/b]

[h1]Q&A :[/h1]
[list]
[*][h2]What is the probability of a key spawning on a vehicle door?[/h2]
It is 1/4 probability compared to the spawn to the glove box. Of course, spawning keys on the door is not a feature of this mod, but of vanilla. This mod only reteives keys that have already spawn on the door.

[*][h2]Is it possible to retrieve the key from the door without using this mod?[/h2]
Yes, it's possible. If a key is spawned on the door, it can be retrieved when trying to enter through a locked door. However, there is no notification, and the key is only silently added to your inventory.

[*][h2]Do I need to try entering through every door to retrieve the key from the door?[/h2]
No, as long as the key is spawned on the door of that car, the key can be retrieved by entering through any door.

[*][h2]Does it work even if it's enabled in the middle of the save data? Or do I need a new game?[/h2]
This mod makes it easier to get keys spawned on doors, rather than having them spawned on doors. Therefore, a new game is not required.
[/list]

[h1]Options :[/h1]
These options are available with [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2169435993]Mod Options[/url] in B41.
[list]
[*]Finding Message Notification : Can specify whether to enable or disable.
[*]Sound Volume (Found) : You can adjust the sound volume when you find the key on the door.
[/list]

[h1]Known Issues :[/h1]
[list]
[*]Nothing
[/list]

[h1]Future plans :[/h1]
[list]
[*]Bug fix (if it exists)
[/list]

[h1]Release Notes :[/h1]
[list]
[*]v1.5 - Compatible with Build 42.
[*]v1.4 - Added Sound Options.
[*]v1.3 - Added ranslation (ES).
[*]v1.2 - Added sound effect file for replacement.
[*]v1.1 - Added ranslation (KO, PTBR, TH).
Please refer to "Change Notes" for the older history.
[/list]

[code]
If you enjoy this mod and would like to support its development, you can do so through the services below. Your support helps cover the cost of renting a dedicated server for multiplayer testing, as well as AI service subscriptions used for development - expenses I personally pay for every month, totaling over $50. Thanks to your support, I can continue improving, maintaining, and expanding this mod for everyone.

[url=https://ko-fi.com/pepepepepeil][img]https://imgur.com/y5Ih4mO.png[/img][/url]
[/code]

Mod ID: P4OnTheDoor

publishedfiledetails.title: One of Us
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]Knox Infection[/h1]
You are one of the few survivors, but, life being life, you got bitten. Why not make the most out of it? With this mod, at a certain point after being infected, zombies will start to see you like one of their own!

[h1]How does it work?[/h1]
As soon as you get infected with the zombie virus, regardless of how, you will begin turning into a zombie just like normal. However, starting at a certain, customizable point, you will become indistinguishable to the zombies!
You can also set a random chance of a zombie being able to spot you even if you are infected, and also a minimum distance you have to be in order to blend in!

publishedfiledetails.title: Open Ammo Boxes While Walking
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
B42.13 (Multiplayer update) Note: Only the vanilla version is available. The mod now makes the following tweaks:
- Allows ammo boxes and containers to be packed in the dark.
- For some reason, there's a small chance that the crafting recipe will do nothing when completed. You can just re-do it and it should go through.

Please also note that the devs of this game have been refactoring the crafting system during the B42 unstable beta, so each update tends to break this mod ;-;

------

This is a basic mod that edits the crafting recipes for opening ammo boxes to allow you to walk while opening ammo boxes. It also allows you to put ammo back into boxes while walking. Running and sprinting will still interrupt the action.

Includes 7 versions to support the many gun mods that exist :)
1) Vanilla
2) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2297098490]Arsenal/Brita[/url]
3) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2256623447]Firearms B41[/url]
4) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/nocapitals/myworkshopfiles/?appid=108600]Vanilla Firearms Expanded[/url]
5) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2874163136]CJ Firearms Expansion[/url]
6) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3077078907]Guns of 93[/url]
7) [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3233647204]Rain's Firearms[/url]

For those using the non-vanilla versions, make sure that this mod loads AFTER Arsenal's/B41/VFE/CJ's mod. Otherwise, this mod will be overwritten and do nothing. Have trouble with the mod order? Usually, switching this mod off and then back on will fix it.

Enjoy!

publishedfiledetails.title: Paint Your Ride
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Tired of boring uniform colors? Want something shiny? Get ready, we're about to Paint Your Ride!

[h2]Features[/h2]
[list]
[*]25 spray paint colors
[*]white paint + 7 tinting paints to mix any color
[*]vehicle sanding and priming
[*]spray and tinting paint catalogues
[/list]
[b]Only civilian vehicles[/b] from the vanilla game and supported mods can be painted.

[h2]How To[/h2]
Before you paint vehicles you need to learn how to do that. Read Autostyle Magazine - Vehicle Painting: volume 1 teaches how to paint vehicles, volume 2 - how to mix paint. Mechanics (or any other) skill doesn't matter.

Then you need to do the following:
[olist]
[*]Wash your car if it's dirty.
[*]Uninstall all windows from it.
[*][b]Sand vehicle:[/b] remove old paint and rust. Requires Wire Brush.
[*][b]Prime vehicle:[/b] prepare surface for new paint. Requires Spray Primer.
[*][b]Paint Your Ride![/b] You have two options:
  [list]
  [*]Paint with ready-to-use sprays - 25 predefined colors for you. Find HSV Spray Paint Catalogue to preview the available colors;
  [*]Mix any color you want.
  In order to do that you need Automotive Paint White and any Tinting Paints (7 available, or you can paint with just clear white paint), and, of course, Spray Gun (requires battery to operate) to apply. Find HSV Tinting Paint Catalogue for tints proportions for some colors.
  [/list]
[/olist]

Every step (except washing) requires personal protective equipment. One of the following:
[list]
[*]Dust Mask and Safety Goggles;
[*]Gas Mask;
[*]Nuclear Biochemical Mask.
[/list]
Safety first!

[h3]Paint amounts[/h3]
One paint can has 10 units of paint.
The larger the car, the more paint is required. Ranges from 21 (2.1 cans) to 45 units (4.5 cans) for the vanilla vehicles.
One tinting paint bottle has 100 units of paint.
To mix a color you can use up to 100 units of each tint. You can achieve same color with different tints combinations.

[h3]Items spawn[/h3]
Spray and tinting paints are distributed mostly in boxes: open them to get up to 4 spray cans of one color or up to 8 tints (6 different + 2 black). There is a chance to get full cans/bottles.

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
Game version: [b]Build 41.60+ (works only in single for now)[/b]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2675429658]1970 Ford Mustang Boss 302[/url]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1510950729]Filibuster Rhymes' Used Cars![/url]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2661819460]Ford Crown Victoria 1999 (beta)[/url]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2664866820]Nissan 240sx (beta)[/url]
[url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2698931985]Nissan Skyline GTR R34[/url]

[h3]For modders[/h3]
If you create recolorable vehicles and want them to be supported by this mod, see [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2281256511/3198118671848972118/]this discussion[/url].

This mod is translated into:
[list]
[*]English, Russian
[*]Brazilian Portuguese - by ZL18k
[*]French - by Bobitec
[*]German - by 0xM4RC3L
[*]Polish - by Eggon
[*]Spanish - by Siowar
[/list]
You know other languages or want to correct an existing translation? [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/2281256511/3001047413720483356/]Look here for the information[/url].

[b]Thanks to Aiteron[/b] for the ideas!

[img]https://i.imgur.com/cKVBRWw.gif[/img]

publishedfiledetails.title: Paint Your Ride - Multiplayer Fix
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Multiplayer fix for [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2281256511]Paint Your Ride[/url].

This mod is not a standalone; you also need to subscribe to the original mod.

The fix was just a single extra copy-pasted line in four functions. I don't know why no one did this before.


[b]Known issue:[/b]
The car painting "state" is not synchronized to the server. In other words, the whole painting process (sanding, priming, painting) of a car must be done by only one player and without logging out of the server. The risk otherwise is having to restart the whole process.
For example, if you had a car primed and ready for the final painting step but then disconnect, you would have to re-sand and re-prime the car (which cost additional paint cans).
This does not affect the result, though; only the process. As long as you do all the steps in a single session then the color of the car will synchronize as expected.
Could I fix this problem? Who knows? The truth is that I'm too lazy. Fix it yourself and publish it as a newer, shinier, better mod.


Workshop ID: 3119830420
Mod ID: PaintYourRideMPFIX

publishedfiledetails.title: Plumb Helper PLUS
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1][b]ABOUT B42[/b][/h1]
When TIS released a major update, my B42 version broke. I will indeed return to modding B42, but would like for it to get to a state that I consider "stable enough to mod" (which I think is fast-approaching). I'm sorry for the inconvenience, and thank you for your understanding. 

[i]After these messages, we'll be right back![/i]

[h2] Check out the infomercials! [/h2]
[url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fBXSlMIrL60]Plumb Helper [/url]
[url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EUEtNrG2Dsg]Plumb Helper PLUS[/url]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/TKWRvF9.png[/img]
[h1][b][i] A SIMPLE TOOL FOR  A SIMPLE JOB! [/i][/b][/h1]

With a [b]pipe wrench[/b] in your inventory, an option will appear in your context menu. The first option will activate a cursor that shows where water sources are and whether or not the tile is considered "Indoors" [i](the two requirements for plumbing sinks).[/i]

If the Kentucky Water Supply is still active and your Find Rain Collectors cursor is on a sink that can be plumbed to it, a new icon of a city with a water drop will appear.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/CNAVxdK.gif[/img]

[h1][b][i]BUT WAIT, THERE'S MORE![/i][/b][/h1]

Now there's a [i]SECOND OPTION![/i] Select it to activate a cursor to find your faucets one floor below! No more running up and down stairs trying to figure out where that sink is!
Sink to new levels with Plumb Helper Plus!

[img]https://i.imgur.com/P0PT6l7.gif[/img]

[h3]Tooltips:[/h3]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/z1ynTgO.png[/img]
[b]^The Find Rain Collectors tooltip will show whether you're currently indoors[/b] 
[i](only checks when you open the context menu- does not update in real-time to preserve performance)[/i]
[b]and whether the KY Water Supply is active[/b] 
[i](sandbox option to disable if you find it unimmersive)[/i]

[img]https://i.imgur.com/hSOk5Y5.png[/img]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/TKWRvF9.png[/img]
[h2][b] Frequently Asked Questions: [/b][/h2]
[list]


[*][h3][b]Does it work in multiplayer?[/b][/h3] 
  Indeed it does!

[*][h3][b]Does it work with a controller?[/b][/h3] 
  You know it!

[*][h3][b]I found a bug/ran into a problem, what should I do?[/b][/h3] 
 Report it [url=https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/filedetails/discussion/3146046428/4139439249275543591/]here[/url]!

[*][h3][b]What's new?[/b][/h3] 
 Check out [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/changelog/3146046428]the changelog[/url]!

[*][h3][b]Can I put this in my server's modpack?[/b][/h3] 
  Sure! (Credit and links appreciated)

[*][h3][b]Are there any known incompatibilities?[/b][/h3] 
  Not that I'm aware of. Tested with over 350 mods both online and in solo! 

[*][h3][b]Is it safe to add or remove mid-save?[/b][/h3] 
  Yes! The option to show the KY Water Supply status is enabled by default, but can be changed at any time using a mod like [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2894296454] Change Sandbox Options (by Star) [/url]

[*][h3][b]The Texture looks bad, what gives?[/b][/h3] 
  If you want cleaner textures, you'll need to disable "Texture Compression" in the Display settings (this also removes the problem of the floor having a visible grid at different zoom levels). This may come at a performance cost, unfortunately.
[/list]
[img]https://i.imgur.com/TKWRvF9.png[/img]
[h2] Translations: [/h2]
Since there were very few lines, I went ahead and translated it into every language PZ is available in. This was done using Google and DeepL, so the results may not be great, but it should be usable. Any improvements are welcome and appreciated. 
Thank you to juni2335 for reminding me about encoding and fixing the Korean translation!
Thank you to Inferno for fixing the Spanish translation!
Thank you to toomanynights for fixing the Russian translation! Check out his excellent QOL mod [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3162507273]Dig Worms[/url]!

[img]https://i.imgur.com/TKWRvF9.png[/img]
[h1][i]Thanks for stopping by, and stay tuned for more mods![/i][/h1]
Don't forget to rate! It helps the mod more than you'd think!

[b]Tips are never expected but always accepted!
And feel free to reach out for commission work, I'd love to make your PZ dreams come true![/b]

publishedfiledetails.title: Plumbing
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]What's new[/h1]

[b]April 28 - You can now repair or disassemble the pump. Sinks can now store more water.
April 04 - Bugfix, check release notes for more info.
March 29 - Use pipe wrench (instead of propane torch) to build or remove pipes.
March 21, 2024 - You can now pump water FROM rain collectors.
March 13, 2024 - You can now connect whole buildings by connecting pipe to edge of the building. [/b]

[h1] Plumbing Mod [/h1]

This mod lets you build a network of pipes to obtain water or fuel for various needs. You can transport water from rivers, lakes, wells, rain collectors and deliver it directly to barrels, connect whole houses to restore water after shutdown and also to irrigate farms. You can also transport fuel from gas stations to metal barrels. 

I spend hundreds of hours with this mod just for my pleasure. And if you like my work, you can [url=/ko-fi.com/M4M7SGR4P][img]https://storage.ko-fi.com/cdn/kofi2.png?v=3[/img][/url]

It is OK to add this mod in the middle of the game. It works for single and multiplayer games.

[h1] How does this work [/h1]

[h2] Pumps [/h2]
Every network must have at least one pump. Pumps are the only means of moving the water of fuel. In particular, this means, that the liquid will not move from barrels to anywhere else without a pump

[h3]Building a pump[/h3]
To build a pump, you need to access the "Plumbing" menu from the main context menu. The menu will be accessible once you have read Metalworking Magazine vol 2. 

Pumps must be build next to water or fuel sources. The only water sources pump can work with are: a water tile (lake or river), a well or a rain collector barrel. Fuel sources are gas pumps. 

Once you have built the pump, select "Attach water source" or "Attach fuel source".  Water tiles (lakes, rivers) will provide with tainted water, wells with fresh water. 

Now you can click "Pump info" to see details about the pump. 

[h3]Powering the pump[/h3]
In order to function, the pump needs electricity. You can use a power generator, but Immersive Solar Arrays mod will also work. Global power before blackout will not work. A running pump will consume 0.09L/h of fuel. When you open "Generator info" the pump will be listed as "Other". Click "Turn on" to start the pump. 

[h3]Pump efficiency[/h3]
The pump you built, will have a starting efficiency depending on you metalworking skill. You will get 10% of efficiency for every skill level. Pump efficiency will very slowly decrease over time, lowering the volume of liquid pumped. 100% efficiency is 12L/10 mins. Pumps below 20% will be additionally susceptible to emergency stops or in rare cases will start fire. Never build pumps close to your base!  A 100% healthy pump needs several months to wear out completely. Repair the pump to regain the efficiency.

[h3]Water filtering[/h3]
By default, a pump provides with tainted water. If you want the pump to deliver drinkable water, you can click "Add Charcoal Filter" if you have charcoal in your inventory. Water filter will be slowly used over time. Use pump info to see the current status of the filter. Water is filtered as long the status of the filter is above 0%. When pumping fuel, filters do not play any role. 

[h2] Collectors[/h2]
Collectors are objects that have the ability of storing liquids. This mod has an explicit list of in-game vanilla object that are recognized as water collectors. This list includes small and big rain collector barrels, sinks, baths, toilets and washing machines. To collect fuel, build a Fuel Tank. To fill the collector a pipe must reach to it.

[h2] Connecting Buildings[/h2]
If the pipe reaches edge of the building, then all water collectors on every level of that building will be filled. The water will fill collectors one by one until full, not paralelly. Self-built houses cannot be connected. Each building must be connected individually, because building are ends of the network and will not relay water further. "Plumb" option is not necessary at all. If you pump tainted water, it will also be tainted in the sinks.

[h2] Pipes [/h2]
Pipes are the only means of transportation liquid from pumps to collectors. Pipes must be placed in a consecutive manner forming a pipeline. No other objects will relay liquid. In particular, this means, that if there is a barrel between the pipes, if will receive the water, but it will not relay it any further. Similarly a connected building is the end of the network and can't relay water to other structures.

[h3]Building pipes[/h3]
To build a pipe, use the "Plumbing" menu again.

The pipes you place will adjust visually automatically. The first pipe will be a cross, but the next one you place next to it will fix both pipes. You can build a simple network, or network with multiple forks, you can even make a loop but then you computer may explode. 

Pipes can be built on multiple floors. Check the screenshot gallery how to achieve this. Just place the pipes in the shown order.

If you build a pipe close to a collector object, the pipe should snap to it correctly. If that does not happen it means that the object is not recognized as a collector. 

To remove a pipe click "Remove Pipe", you will get you pipe item back. 

[h3]Water movement[/h3]
If the pump is on, and the pipes are connected to it, liquid will start flowing. Every in-game 10 minutes, liquid will be transported to the end(s) of the network. 

If you networks forks, the liquid amount will be split. The split may be unequal in certain situations. For example if there are 13 units of water to be split across 2 pipes, one will receive 6 units, and the other will get 7 units. If there are too many consecutive splits, some pipes will not receive liquid at all, that happens when
there is only 1 unit of liquid that needs to be split. 

You network can split liquid, but also join streams from multiple pipelines. Note, that the pipe however has a maximum throughput that is equal to the amount of five 100% efficient pumps. Joining streams strategy is effective if you want to transport large amount of liquid from multiple pumps using one shared pipeline.

If you mix gasoline and water in one network, the network will not work. 

[h2]Valves[/h2]
Every pipeline intersection has valves that can be changed. Valves are present on each ending or a pipe, so a T-shape pipe intersection has 3 valves, and the cross shape pipe has 4 valves. Valves are named by their placement, so there can be north, east, west and south valve. 

Each of these valves can operate in two modes
[list]
[*] Two-way valve - will allow liquid to flow in both directions
[*] One-way valve (receiver) - will allow liquid to flow in one direction, it will allow liquid only to come in this pipe, and not come out to pipe connected to this end. 
[/list]
Valve settings will have impact on how liquid is split or merged on intersections. 

To access valve information use context menu on the intersection and click "Valve info". This information is updated not immediately, but every 10 in-game minutes.

Similarly, to change valve settings, use context menu on the intersection and click "Change valves".

[h2]Farms irrigation[/h2]
Farms built one square next to straight pipes will be watered automatically. Some portion of transported water will be used but only if the plant needs it. It works on vanilla farms,
but also with other mods using standard farming system such as Mo Crops or Farmable Trees. 

[h1] Troubleshooting [/h1]
[olist]
[*] If there are problems, launch the game in a debug mode. In context menu, there will be more options you can use for debugging such as a map of your network.
[*] Incompatibility with Hydrocraft, fix: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3152142389
[/olist]

Workshop ID: 3118159023
Mod ID: Plumbing

publishedfiledetails.title: Post Flag
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[h1]Overview[/h1]
Raise or lower the post flag depending on the contents of the post inside.
[b][i]Confirmed to work with Build 41/42.[/i][/b]

[h1]Features :[/h1]
If there are items inside, the post flag will stand.
[img]https://imgur.com/Afi0rKc.png[/img]
If there are no items inside, the post flag will remain lowered.
[img]https://imgur.com/iaxHjKz.png[/img]
[h3][b][img]https://imgur.com/bfxjRMM.png[/img] In the case of Mid-Save, a post on a map that has already been loaded is displayed with the flag down, regardless of whether there are items inside or not. In this case, moving the item in the container will return it to the normal display.[/b][/h3]

[h1]Known Issues :[/h1]
[list]
[*]There was a bug where, if a flagged post was destroyed by a vehicle, both the original graphic and the destroyed graphic would remain. This bug has already been fixed, but in saves from before the fix, flagged posts may still appear this way.
[img]https://imgur.com/yhtS8DK.png[/img]
In such cases, you can remove the original graphic by taking items out of the destroyed post.
[img]https://imgur.com/uO4QfWG.png[/img]
[/list]

[h1]Future plans :[/h1]
[list]
[*]Nothing
[/list]

[h1]Remarks :[/h1]
[list]
[*]This mod requires that "DISPLAY > Textures > Double-sized Textures" in GAME OPTIONS is ON. If this option is OFF, the correct size will not be displayed. I don't plan to support this option now, but I will consider it if there are enough requests.
[*]I know the original meaning of the post flag is "There is mail to be delivered", but in this mod, I used this flag to easily identify the contents of the mailbox.
[/list]

[h1]Release Notes :[/h1]
[list]
[*]v1.3 - Compatible with Build 42.
[*]v1.2 - Fixed for remaining graphics.
[*]v1.1 - Temporary fix for remaining graphics.
[*]v1.0 - New release.
[/list]

[code]
If you enjoy this mod and would like to support its development, you can do so through the services below. Your support helps cover the cost of renting a dedicated server for multiplayer testing, as well as AI service subscriptions used for development - expenses I personally pay for every month, totaling over $50. Thanks to your support, I can continue improving, maintaining, and expanding this mod for everyone.

[url=https://ko-fi.com/pepepepepeil][img]https://imgur.com/y5Ih4mO.png[/img][/url]
[/code]

publishedfiledetails.title: Radio connected to the grid B41 (MP and SP).
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Multiplayer and singleplayer compatible.

This mod lets you to connect all kinds of vanilla radios, walkietalkies, HAM radios to wall outlet, so there are no need of batteries. Perfect way to listen to Emergency broadcast or Survivor radio mod.

Electrical level 1 is needed and few resources (2x electronic scrap, 1 electric wire) and you need to have screwdriver.
To convert to use grid power you need to have radio in inventory (or container), convert and then place down where are electricity (grid still working, or in range of generator).

[b]Remove batery first before connecting to grid[/b]. Mod doesn't check if there is battery in radio/walkietalkie/HAM radio, so you can loose the batery. Battery check will be added later.

[b]Remove CD first before connecting to grid[/b]. Mod doesn't check if there is CD in radio, so you will loose it.
[b]Premium radio connected to grid dont have ability to play CD's[/b]. 

Connecting (converting) changes presets on radio, so you will have to add presets again (for eg Emergency broadcast). You can use [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2735294987]Radio Frequency Manager by Kaldo[/url] to save/load radio presets.

Currently there is no way to dissasemble connected radios/walkietalkies. That funcionality will be added later, to get same items one would get using vanilla dissasemble option.

Mod can be turned on mid game since it only adds craftable items and doesn't need recipe journals. [b]Turning mod off will make your radios connected to grid dissapear.[/b]
Tested on 41.78 and 41.65

If you enjoy this mod, please consider liking it and favourite it.
[img=https://i.imgur.com/l2s0ddf.gif][/img]

[hr][/hr]

Workshop ID: 2001719506
Mod ID: RadioToGrid

publishedfiledetails.title: Rain Wash
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
Rain washes blood and dirt away. Including the character's body, clothing, equipped bags, equipped and attached weapons, the vehicle your character is in and vehicle attached to it or last used vehicle and vehicle attached to it.

[h2]How it works[/h2]
[h3]Body, clothing, bags, weapons[/h3]
When it's raining and your character is outside (and not in a car, of course) the mentioned things will slowly be cleaned. The process begins as soon as a body part* or item becomes wet: some of its blood is turned into dirt, then the dirt is washed away.

Not everything is cleaned at the same time! Notice how wetness is increased: top layer of clothing / uncovered body parts -> inner layer of clothing -> covered body parts. It's a vanilla mechanic.

How fast an item or body will be cleaned depends on rain intensity and the item's or body's wetness. Not all dirt will be washed off clothes! Depending on the water resistance of the clothing, up to 20% of dirt will remain. Not all dirt will be washed off your character! Up to 10% of dirt will remain.

[h3]Vehicles[/h3]
The vehicle your character is in and the vehicle attached to it will be cleaned.

The last used vehicle and the vehicle attached to it will be cleaned after your character leaves it too. This works as long as your character is within approximately 100 squares of these vehicles. After reloading the save you need to enter/exit again for this to work.

[h3]Wash In The Rain[/h3]
When your character is outside and it's raining right click and you'll see the "Wash In The Rain" option. Works like "Wash Yourself" but slower and your character will take off all clothes (and put them on after finishing).

[h2]Options[/h2]
If you want to configure the mod, you need to install and enable the [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2169435993]Mod Options[/url] mod.
Go to [i]Options -> Mods -> Rain Wash[/i].

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
Game version: [b]Build 41.56+ (MP supported)[/b]
This mod is compatible with:
- any mods that add clothing, bags and weapons;
- any mods that add/change character traits;
- any mods that add vehicles.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/cKVBRWw.gif[/img]

[b]Developing mods takes a lot of my free time and I would appreciate it if you could give me more motivation to create quality content by supporting me:[/b]

[url=https://discord.gg/pQWytxRmUJ][img]https://i.imgur.com/MQBcH2s.png[/img][/url]

[b]My other mods:[/b]

publishedfiledetails.title: Realistic Dashboard & Gauges [MP] [Beta]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
This beta version mod adds a realistic vehicle dashboard for Project Zomboid B41/B42/MP. [b] SAFE FOR EXISTING SAVES [/b]

Workshop ID: 3635591071
Mod ID: RealisticDash

[h1]Patch note[/h1]
[list]
[*]1/7/2026: Compatibility fix for Autostar Motor Club mod. Small artistic changes (see change note).
[*]1/2/2026: Added option to change stain and crack opacity (only for B42).
[*]1/1/2026: Added HD texture for high resolution screens (only for B42). [b] For users of 4k / 2160p screens, please go to OPTIONS -> MODS to select the 2x size texture. [/b]
[/list]

[h1] Tip for smoother animation[/h1]
In Options → Display, increase UI Render FPS (higher = smoother UI). Recommended value is 60 fps.

[h1]Features and highlights[/h1]
[list]
[*]Full radio, A/C, locks, and window control on dashboard.

[*]Dashboard for driver AND front row passenger.

[*]Physics-based gauge needle motion.

[*]Gauge backlit illumination.

[*]Dynamic stain and crack effect. (Affected by driver seat and windshield condition)

[*]Revamped warning lights. (with startup bulb-check)

[*]Dynamic cranking voltage sag effect. (Affected by battery remaining charge)

[*]Two different radio ui for ValueTech/Premium Technologies radio.

[/list]
[h1]Version/ MP compatability[/h1]
Tested on Project Zomboid B41 (Singleplayer), B42 (Singleplayer), and B42 (Multiplayer). This mod is safe for existing saves.
This is a beta version — bugs and issues may occur.

[h1]Mod compatability[/h1]
This mod respects vanilla game logic and does not currently rely on any custom data saving.
It should be compatible with most non-dashboard mods that also follow vanilla behavior. Mods tested being compatible (but not limited to):
[list]
[*] TV and Radio Reinvented

[*] True Music

[*] Project Summer Cars (Please load Project Summer Cars before this mod. However its thermometer and warning lights may be covered. This can be patched in future update)

[*] KI5 car mods

[/list]

[h1]How to use[/h1]
This mod does not require explicit setup. It should replace vanilla dashboard UI automatically when mod is installed. [b] For B42 users of 4k / 2160p screens, please go to OPTIONS -> MODS to select the 2x size texture. [/b]
For radio and warning lights, please see the flyers:
[img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/17835549219215167773/FE3DA36A458F242C68E47DB2B0279D14F7B38B57/?imw=637&imh=358&ima=fit&impolicy=Letterbox&imcolor=%23000000&letterbox=true[/img]

[img]https://images.steamusercontent.com/ugc/16101101754868648390/B7EE2D261590F544EE73CAA77C8A47250EF3F34B/?imw=637&imh=358&ima=fit&impolicy=Letterbox&imcolor=%23000000&letterbox=true[/img]

[h1]Planned updates[/h1]
[list]
[*] Emergency light control dashboard for emergency vehicles

[*] Different dashboard design for different vehicles

[*] Ham radio interface

[/list]

[h1]Bug report[/h1]
If you encounter a bug, please include your build number, game mode, and name of other mods if there is a compatibility issue.

[h1]Author’s Note[/h1]

Hi everyone! I’m the creator of this mod. I’m originally from Nanjing, China, and I’m currently studying in the United States. Cars have always been my biggest passion, especially the small design and mechanical details. Beyond dashboard and gauges, I’m also interested in hybrid powertrain architectures and how real-world engineering choices shape driving experiences.

This is my first game mod, and it’s still in beta, so bugs and compatibility issues are to be expected. I’ll continue to actively maintain it and push updates as issues come up, and I truly appreciate any feedback you’d like to share.

Thank you for downloading this mod, giving it a try, or even just taking the time to read this note. I hope it adds a bit more immersion and enjoyment to your apocalyptic driving experience!

publishedfiledetails.title: Reorder The Hotbar [B42/41]
publishedfiledetails.file_description:
[b]Reorder The Hotbar[/b] is a mod that allows you to customize the order of the items in your hotbar.

Simply drag and drop the hotbar slots to rearrange them to your liking.
This mod also adds the ability to click on the hotbar slots as an alternative to pressing the hotkeys.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/p7Fv1Z6.gif[/img]

[h1]FAQ[/h1]
[h3]Clicking on some slots doesn't work![/h3]
Clicking on the hotbar slots relies on a keyboard hotkey being set. Ensure you have mapped hotkeys for the slots beyond the vanilla 1-5.

[h3]How do you get all those slots?[/h3]
Other mods are required. I'm using the tier 3 ALICE backpack from [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2335368829]Authentic Z[/url] in the second preview.

[h3]If you enjoy my mods, consider supporting me on Ko-fi![/h3]
[url=https://ko-fi.com/notloc][img]https://i.imgur.com/jLt3Dyv.png[/img][/url]
Or, just come check out some previews of what I'm working on next!
